5: the malison coils low behind a curtain of vines
scales flash green then vanish into shadow again
a forked tongue tastes the air before the strike
poison drips slow from a fang aimed at your neck
torches flare as the serpent lord rears up tall
the yuan ti hisses and slides behind cracked stone
a rapier flicks out from the dark twisting coils
your blade meets scale and sparks off with a hiss
green eyes blink slow from the shrine of old gold
the coil tightens then snaps open in one strike
a hood of scales spreads wide before the lunge
poison hums along the blade as it swings for you
the boss rises from a nest of carved stone snakes
a hiss rolls through the trees like a warning bell
scales click on stone as the malison circles you
the serpent lord flicks its tail and strikes fast
a green blur drops from the vines above your head
the malison smiles with a mouth full of old venom
torchlight catches fangs bared in the dark forest
a coil wraps the shrine steps in loose green scale
the fight opens with a hiss and a flash of steel
poison mist curls low across the cracked stone floor
your dodge is quick but the tail catches your boot
a rapier spins once then aims for your open guard
the malison rears back and calls for its snakes
green scale glints under torches near the old shrine
a strike lands and the serpent recoils with a hiss
the boss slithers behind broken pillars for cover
poison drips from a fang as the coil pulls tight
your blade catches the rapier in a shower of sparks
the malison hisses a word and the forest goes still
a shadow with scales slides past the shrine gate
torches gutter as the serpent lord looms closer
the fight turns fast when the coil finds your arm
green fangs snap shut just short of your shoulder
a hiss and a lunge and the malison is on you again
poison stings where the fang grazed your sleeve
the serpent king coils twice before the next strike
your parry rings off scale in the dim forest light
a rapier thrust slides past your guard and misses
the malison rears with both arms raised for a spell
green light gathers at the tip of a raised claw
a firebolt cracks the air near the shrine steps
the coil recoils then whips low toward your legs
poison hums in the air after the fang draws back
your blade finds the gap between two thick scales
the malison hisses low and slides into the dark
a scale shield turns your strike with a dull clank
torches throw long shadows across the shrine floor
the serpent lord strikes twice before you can move
green coils tangle the roots near the old altar
a final hiss echoes as the malison falls at last
poison fades slow from the blade after the win
the forest goes quiet once the last scale stops

5: wind cuts low across the gorse and the wolf goes still
a dire wolf slips between two standing stones and waits
you hear claws on stone before you see the grey shape
sheep bones scatter as the pack circles the old cairn
heather bends flat where something heavy just crouched
a low growl rolls down the hill before the charge
fangs flash white against the dark shape of the wolf
the hill goes quiet and that quiet is the warning
a shadow breaks from the gorse and the chase begins
you plant your feet as the wolf closes the last steps
cold wind carries the smell of wet fur and old blood
the wolf circles wide then cuts straight for your leg
stone teeth of the cairn cast long shadows at dusk
a howl answers from somewhere past the next ridge
you feel the ground shake as heavy paws hit the turf
the wolf snaps at empty air where your arm just was
tall grass hides three more shapes moving low and fast
a blade swings wide and the wolf twists just clear
you plant a boot on the cairn stone and swing hard
the pack spreads out to pin you against the ridge
grey fur bristles as the wolf reads your next move
wind howls through the gap where old bones still lie
you duck low as jaws snap shut just past your ear
the wolf lunges and you roll clear across wet grass
thunder of paws grows louder behind the standing stone
a snarl builds low in its throat before the leap
you swing hard and the wolf yelps and falls back
the hill goes dark as clouds roll over the ridge
claws rake stone as the wolf scrambles up the slope
you brace against a cairn as the pack closes in
a second wolf slides out from behind the gorse bush
teeth close on empty cloth as you twist free again
the wolf backs off then circles for another angle
cold rain slicks the grass where the wolf lost its grip
you hear bone crack under a heavy grey paw nearby
a low ridge hides the pack until the very last step
the wolf finally breaks and bolts across open moor
you land a hit right along its scarred grey flank
wind drops and the whole hill seems to hold its breath
the wolf snarls once then goes for your open side
a cairn stone shifts loose as you brace your weight
you spot the alpha wolf watching from the far ridge
jaws snap shut an inch from your trailing boot heel
the wolf drags low through heather to stay hidden
you swing again and this time the blow lands true
a whole pack of eyes glow faint in the fading light
the wolf skids on wet stone and loses its footing
you feel hot breath before you see the grey muzzle
the hill wind carries a long low howl toward you
claws dig deep into turf as the wolf pushes off hard
you step back onto solid stone and set your stance
the wolf circles the cairn twice before it commits
a final lunge and the wolf goes down in the grass
quiet returns to the hill once the last wolf yields
the harpy circles above the wind scoured hill
a cairn of stones marks where the harpy waits
gorse and heather hide the harpy from your eyes
wind claws at the ragged cliffs where it perches
sheep bones scatter across the old stone shelf
the harpy screams and drops from a heather ledge
tumbled stones crack beneath your boots tonight
its wings beat hard against the scoured hilltop
you climb the cairn while the harpy circles low
talons rake the air above the windy ridge
a shriek rolls down across the tumbled stones
the harpy dives and you duck behind a cairn
bones and heather crunch beneath your steps
wind howls across the hill where the harpy waits
you brace as feathers slice through the cold air
the cairn topples and stones roll down the slope
its claws grip the rock ledge above the gorse
you dodge as the harpy screams and swoops again
heather bends flat beneath the beating wings
the harpy perches high on the tallest stone
sheep bones crack under your boots as you run
wind drags heavy clouds across the green hill
you strike as the harpy folds its ragged wings
stones shift and tumble down the steep hillside
the harpy circles wide then drops like a stone
gorse scratches your arms as you climb higher

9: Cut one head off the hydra & watch two more burst up from the stump (ugh)!
Roll 2d6+3 against AC 14: can you cauterize the neck before it regrows?
Seven heads snap at once! Do you have a plan, or just a torch and hope?
DC 15 to keep your footing on the slick mud (the mere is rising fast)!
Hit for 4d4+2 and the wound seals - no regrowth, finally, a clean cut!

Will the last head fall before the reeds swallow the torch's light?

Cauterize now or count on losing ground (each new head hits for 1d6+1).

Every torch thrown costs a turn: is the trade worth stopping regrowth?

Reeds part as the beast surges forward: 60 hp left, heads still multiplying.
Bubble after bubble breaks the surface (something huge moves beneath).
Strike the central head for 3d6: it's the only one that won't regrow!
Cold steel bites deep but two new heads erupt (was that worth it)?
The party circles the mere: who holds the torch while the rest swing?
Ribs show through torn hide at 20% hp - one more good hit should do it!
A neck whips low & nearly drags a friend under the black water!
Roll for damage: 2d10+4 fire finally cauterizes the worst wound.
Nine heads become ten in a blink (this is not going the way we planned).
The frogs go silent all at once: something bigger just woke up.
Torchlight flickers across scales as the last head rears up (here it comes)!
The hydra thrashes, sinking half its bulk (37 hp left and falling).

Count the heads before you swing (miscount and you'll waste the turn)!
The beast's tail slams the shore: DC 14 or get knocked flat in the muck.
One clean strike (4d6) finally drops the last head for good!
Water churns black as the hydra dives for cover (60% hp remaining).
The final neck rears up alone: one good hit (3d8+2) should end this.
Every head that falls without fire comes back twice as fast (ugh)!
The mere goes still at last: no more heads, no more heat, just steam.
Roll the last d20 of the fight: beat DC 10 and the hydra finally falls!
Cut one head (2d8+4 damage) & watch two more surge up from the stump!
DC 15 save or the mere floods your torch: burn the wound before it grows!
Roll 1d20+5 vs AC 17 - sever clean, then cauterize (or regret it)!
Every neck you cut costs 10% more hp unless the flame seals the gap!
Four heads strike at once: block 2, dodge 1, and pray on the last (DC 14)!
The stump hisses & smokes - quench it now or face 6 heads by dawn!
Swing true (1d12+3) and the water churns black with rotten blood!
Can you land 3 crits before the fifth neck rises from the muck?
Torches drop to 20% oil: burn fast or the heads regrow in the dark!
Slick scales turn your blade (DC 16 to pierce) - aim for the throat!
Two heads down, four to go - is the stump cauterized yet (check now)?
The beast rears up: 90 hp remain & every strike risks a fresh head!
Hold the line! DC 13 vs the tail sweep or lose your footing in the mud!
Fire (2d6) seals what steel (1d10+2) alone will never finish for good!
Half the heads sleep (40% chance) - strike the waking ones first!
One clean cut, one torch, one silent stump: repeat until the water stills!

10: The scree shifts under your boots as the young silver dragon lands on the switchback, wings folding like a closing door. Its breath frosts the air before it even opens its mouth. You brace and swing.

Frost curls off the dragon's snout as it noses along the ridge, hunting your scent. The wind carries ice crystals that sting your face. You duck behind a boulder and time your strike for its next breath.
The monastery bell rings once across the thin air, warning of the dragon circling the glacier below. You climb the last switchback with your shield raised. Its shadow crosses the snow ahead of you.
Scale by scale the dragon uncoils along the ridge, each motion slow as a held breath. Eagles scatter from the cliffs above. You plant your feet on the ice and roll for initiative against the cold.
A gust of frost breath rolls down the pass, freezing the loose scree into a glassy sheet. The dragon watches from the monastery roof, waiting. You steady your grip and start the climb again.
The young dragon perches on the temple stair, wings catching the thin light like hammered silver. Below, the glacier groans. You take a breath, plant your feet, and begin the fight.
Ice cracks under a wingbeat as the dragon lifts off the scree slope, circling once before diving. Cold air burns your lungs at this height. You raise your shield and brace for the strike.
The dragon's roar rolls down the switchback and sets loose stones tumbling toward the glacier. Monks watch from the monastery door, silent. You grip your weapon and climb into the wind.
Frost blooms across the flagstones of the courtyard as the dragon exhales, testing its range. You feel the cold bite through your sleeve. Rolling a natural twenty, you close the distance fast.
High on the pass, the air thins enough that every breath aches, and the dragon seems to breathe it easier than you do. It lands between you and the monastery gate. You choose to fight, not run.
The dragon's tail sweeps a heap of scree across the trail, cutting off your retreat toward the switchback. Eagles wheel overhead, crying warning. You set your stance and wait for its next move.
Cold radiates from the dragon even at a distance, fogging your breath before it opens its jaws. The glacier below cracks like distant thunder. You time your swing to its slow, deliberate stride.
A monk's bell tolls from the monastery as the dragon lands on the frozen courtyard stones, wings dusting frost into the air. You feel the temperature drop around your ankles. The fight begins.
The switchback narrows to a ledge just wide enough for one, and the dragon blocks it entirely, snout lowered. Wind screams past the cliff. You grip tight and step forward anyway.
Scree rattles loose beneath the dragon's claws as it turns to face you, frost trailing from its nostrils like smoke. The monastery bell rings faintly below. You roll damage and hold your ground.
The glacier's blue ice throws back the dragon's silver scales in shards of cold light. It watches you climb the last stretch of the pass. You reach the top and swing before it can breathe.
A gust carries frost crystals across the courtyard as the dragon settles on the temple roof, wings half spread. Eagles bank away from the cold. You steady your breathing and begin the fight.
The dragon's breath weapon rakes across the flagstones, leaving a shimmer of frost where your feet were a heartbeat ago. You duck behind a pillar. The monastery holds its breath with you.
Halfway up the switchback, ice slicks the trail and the dragon lands just above you, cutting off the climb. Its eyes track your every step. You grip the rock and prepare to strike upward.
Wind roars through the pass as the young dragon circles once, testing the thermals before it dives toward the monastery courtyard. You raise your shield. The cold bites at your exposed skin.
The dragon lands atop a heap of old snow, claws crunching through the crust, and turns its silver head slowly toward you. Bells ring somewhere below. You grip your weapon and step closer.
Frost feathers the stone archway as the dragon exhales through the monastery gate, testing distance before it commits to the charge. You feel the chill reach your bones. You brace and swing.
High above the glacier, the dragon rides a thermal in slow circles, silver scales flashing against grey cloud. It folds its wings and drops. You set your feet on the ice and wait for the hit.
The switchback trail vanishes under a sudden drift as the dragon's wingbeat kicks up snow. Cold stings your eyes. You hear its claws on stone behind you and turn to meet the strike.
Scree tumbles past your boots toward the glacier as the dragon lands hard on the ridge, frost pluming from its nostrils. The monastery bell answers with a single ring. You charge forward.
The dragon coils along the courtyard wall, tail curling past frozen flagstones, watching you with patient, cold eyes. Eagles cry from the cliffs above. You grip tight and make your move.
Ice crusts the monastery steps where the dragon breathed moments before, and steam rises where your warm blood nearly spilled. You shake off the chill. The fight is far from over.
A wingbeat sends loose scree cascading down the switchback as the dragon lifts, circling for another pass. The thin air makes your chest ache. You plant your feet and ready your shield.
The young dragon lands between two frozen pillars in the courtyard, wings brushing frost onto the stone. Its breath fogs thick in the cold air. You step forward and roll to attack.
Cracks spread across the glacier as the dragon's weight settles onto the ice shelf, testing its footing before the lunge. You feel the ground shift under your boots. You brace hard.
The monastery gate stands open, frost creeping along its hinges where the dragon exhaled last. Somewhere a bell rings twice. You step through the cold and raise your weapon to fight.
Wind cuts sideways across the pass as the dragon banks low, silver wings nearly brushing the scree slope. You duck and feel the frost breath pass just over your head. You strike back.
The dragon's claws leave deep gouges in the frozen courtyard stone as it turns, slow and deliberate, to face you fully. Eagles scream a warning from above. You grip your weapon tighter.
Snow squeaks under your boots as you climb the last switchback, and the dragon rises from behind a boulder, wings snapping open. Cold slams into you. You roll initiative and hold firm.
A heap of shattered ice marks where the dragon first tested its breath against the monastery wall. Steam still rises from the stone. You step past it, weapon ready, toward the courtyard.
The glacier groans under distant weight as the dragon shifts its stance, frost dripping from its jaws like slow rain. You feel the cold settle into your gloves. You swing and connect.
High on the ridge, thin air makes the dragon's roar sound strange and hollow, bouncing off distant stone. Eagles scatter in every direction. You brace your shield and step into the wind.
The dragon perches on the switchback's highest point, wings folded, watching your slow climb with patient cold eyes. Scree slides beneath your boots. You reach the top and attack first.
Frost spreads across the flagstones in a widening circle as the dragon breathes, testing the range before it commits. You feel your fingers stiffen around your weapon. You strike anyway.
The monastery's oldest bell cracks the thin mountain air as the dragon lands in the courtyard, ice forming where its claws touch stone. You grip tight and step forward to meet it.
A sudden gust nearly knocks you off the switchback as the dragon dives past, frost breath missing you by inches. Scree rattles down toward the glacier below. You turn and swing hard.
The dragon curls along the icy ledge, tail brushing loose scree into the void below, and lifts its head to study you. Wind howls through the pass. You set your feet and hold your ground.
Steam rises where the dragon's breath meets warmer stone near the monastery hearth room, fogging the courtyard air. You feel the chill anyway. You raise your weapon and step closer.
The glacier's surface cracks in a long slow line as the dragon lands, testing whether the ice will hold its weight. You feel the tremor through your boots. You brace and strike first.
Wind screams past the cliff edge as the young dragon circles once more, silver scales catching pale light. Eagles dive for cover in the rocks. You plant your feet and ready your shield.
The dragon's claws scrape frost patterns into the courtyard stone as it turns toward you, slow and confident. A distant bell tolls twice. You grip your weapon and step into the cold.
High above the pass, the dragon rides the thin air effortlessly, wings barely moving, while you gasp for breath at altitude. It dives suddenly. You raise your shield just in time.
Scree shifts and slides beneath the dragon's landing, sending small stones bouncing toward the glacier's edge. Its cold eyes fix on you. You grip your weapon and refuse to step back.
The monastery courtyard fills with fog as the dragon's frost breath meets the warmer stone near the gate. You squint through the mist. Something moves and you swing on instinct.
A single eagle cries out from the cliff face as the dragon lands on the switchback below, frost trailing from its wingtips. You feel the temperature drop sharply. You brace to fight.
The dragon's tail curls tight around a frozen pillar in the courtyard as it watches you approach, patient and cold. Bells ring somewhere in the monastery halls. You step forward to strike.
The young dragon slams down onto the switchback, cracking the ice underfoot, and lifts its head to test the wind for your scent. Eagles wheel off in alarm. You grip your shield and stand firm.

10: The Winter Wolf breaks from the whiteout before you even notice the shape moving. Jaws already wide, it lunges hard for your shoulder. Frost seeps from its fur as it moves. You swing before it can pivot fully away.

It circles once, testing the wind and the footing beneath its paws. Then it lunges, teeth flashing like drawn blades. The whole floe seems to hold its breath. You plant your feet and meet the charge head on.

Cold air cracks like a slab of thin ice giving way. The wolf hits the ground hard, spattering slush everywhere. It flattens its ears, gathering itself for more. Then it charges again, faster than before.

Its eyes catch the aurora overhead and burn briefly gold. Cruel and bright, it slips backward into drifting snow. Ice groans beneath both of you as you circle. The wolf cuts off your retreat toward the wreck.
You feel the arctic cold bite straight through your mail. The wolf feels it too, growing sluggish now. Its breath steams a little less each passing round. One more solid hit should finish this fight.
A gust of wind carries the wolf's roar across the wreck. Something deep in the cracking ice answers back. The beast closes the distance faster than expected. Its jaws snap shut on empty air again.
Snow explodes upward as the wolf lands hard nearby. You roll away and feel the whole ice shift. Coming up swinging, your blade is already drawn. The wolf circles, choosing its next line of attack.

The Winter Wolf's fur is chapped white at the collar. Scarred from older fights, it barely flinches at your hit. Cold water seeps up through a crack near your boots. It circles once more, picking a new angle.
It bites down hard on your sleeve, missing flesh by inches. The whole weight of it tries to yank you off balance. You dig your heels into the packed snow. It pivots away faster than you expected.

Somewhere past the wreck the wind howls through a ridge. The wolf answers the sound before turning back to you. You notice its ribs before you notice its eyes. Thin and hungry in the dying arctic light.

The floe cracks once beneath the wolf's shifting weight. For a moment you both freeze completely, listening hard. Ice settles somewhere far below the surface. Then the fight begins again without warning.
Its jaws close on your shield instead of your arm. The impact rattles up through your whole body at once. You shove it back hard across the ice. It backs off three full steps, still circling.
The wolf's breath fogs thick in the freezing air. You swing and miss, blade whistling through empty cold. It uses the opening to lunge again quickly. Ice needles hang heavy from its muzzle.
When it snarls the ice needles click together sharply. Its paws barely make a sound on packed snow. Silence swallows everything except your own breath. Then the wolf strikes again from the side.
A shard of aurora light catches the wolf's eyes. For one second it looks almost strangely curious. You brace hard against the cold wrecked hull. The wolf circles slowly, looking for an opening.
The wolf snaps suddenly at your ankle, missing narrowly. You jump the widening gap in the cracking ice. It lands hard on the far side instead. Wind cuts sideways hard across the whole floe.
The wolf uses that wind, coming from a new direction. Its claws rake across your armor, missing bare skin. The sound echoes flat across the frozen field. It howls once loudly before charging again.
That howl seems to freeze the whole air itself. You feel the ice shift sharply beneath your boots. It forces you back steadily toward open dark water. Its teeth catch your sleeve and tear it open.
Cold air rushes in where the fabric splits wide. The wolf shakes off a hit, fur bristling with frost. It comes at you again, jaws wide open. Snow kicks up hard around its scrambling paws.

It circles wide, cutting a path straight to the wreck. You duck fast under a swing of its snapping jaws. Its breath is hot and sour against your frozen cheek. Its shoulder catches the edge of your raised shield.
The whole floe rings loudly with that hard impact. Ice fog rolls low and thick across the ground. The wolf uses it to vanish and reappear closer. You land a solid hit and it yelps sharply.
It backs away fast, one paw held off the ice. The Winter Wolf circles the wreck twice, sizing you up. Then it finally commits fully to the charge. Its fur looks nearly white against the deep snow.

Only its dark eyes give its position away in whiteout. You brace hard as it leaps the gap between floes. Jaws snap shut a hair from your shoulder blade. It drags one leg, wounded, but still moves fast.

Cracks spread wide under both your feet at once. The fight pushes toward the thinnest part of the floe. The wolf feints left hard, then lunges hard right. It tests your guard with quick, cruel little strikes.
You hear its claws scrape ice before you even see it. A warning half a second before the real attack lands. Its howl rolls out over open water, lonely and vast. Then it turns back fully to the fight.
Frost cracks off your blade as you swing it hard. The bitter cold makes even good steel feel brittle. The wolf's eyes flash gold in the last daylight. It circles once more for one final attack.
You feel the whole wind drop suddenly, the arctic gone still. Right before the wolf finally makes its move. It slips once on a patch of clear ice. You take the opening fast without hesitating at all.

Steam rises off the wolf's flank where your last hit landed. It cools fast in the bitter freezing air. The wolf staggers back hard, breath ragged and slow. It circles once more before the fight finally turns.

The Winter Wolf noticed you first, five full strides off. It closes the distance faster than anything that big should move. You raise your blade hard against the biting wind. The whole floe cracks loudly beneath you both.
Ice cracks like a whip across the whole frozen field. The wolf's shoulder slams into you like a falling slab. You stagger hard but somehow hold your footing. It circles back fast for another pass.
Your breath fogs white and thick in the freezing air. The wolf's breath does too, steady and heavy. Somewhere nearby the ice groans low and long. Then the wolf lunges suddenly without any warning.

You notice the wolf before it notices you, just barely. It flattens against the snow, nearly invisible in white. Then it springs, teeth bared and gold eyes wide. You barely twist clear of its snapping bite.

The wreck's broken hull creaks loudly in the wind behind you. The wolf uses that noise to mask its approach. Its paws stay silent on the hard packed snow. You feel its hot breath before you even see it.

Aurora light ripples green and blue across the whole sky. The wolf pays it no mind, focused only on you. Its jaws snap twice in quick, sharp succession. You parry both strikes with your blade raised high.
The bitter cold has slowed your fingers on the hilt. It has clearly not slowed the wolf down at all. It circles twice, testing the length of your reach. Then it lunges hard for the gap in your guard.
Snow drifts sting hard at your eyes in the wind. The wolf uses that moment to close ground fast. Its shoulder catches you before you can fully turn. You stumble back hard onto the cracking ice.
A long crack splits the floe wide between you both. The wolf leaps it easily without slowing down at all. You brace, plant your feet, and swing hard. The blade catches it clean across the shoulder.

The wolf's fur steams faintly in the bitter cold air. Its breath comes ragged after that last hard exchange. Still it circles, patient, looking for an opening. You match its careful pace, waiting too.

Ice needles rattle loudly off the wrecked hull nearby. The wolf startles hard at the sudden sound, ears flat. You use the half second to close distance fast. Your blade meets fur before it can dodge away.

The whiteout thickens fast, swallowing the whole horizon. Somewhere close the wolf's low growl carries through it. You turn, blade ready, listening hard for its paws. It lunges out of the white without warning.
Frozen mist clings thick to your eyelashes as you wait. The wolf circles just past the edge of sight. Then it breaks cover, jaws snapping at your arm. You twist away fast and counter hard.
The Winter Wolf's howl echoes off the cracked ice shelf. It sounds almost lonely in the vast white silence. Then it turns, eyes gold, and charges straight at you. You brace hard and meet it head on.
Your boots crunch on refrozen snow as you circle slow. The wolf mirrors your steps exactly, patient and cruel. It waits for you to commit to a swing first. When you finally do, it strikes back hard.
Wind howls low and long across the open ice field. The wolf uses that sound to cover its approach. It comes from your blind side, fast and silent. You barely raise your shield in time to block.

The wreck's frozen ropes creak somewhere high above you. The wolf ignores them, focused entirely on the fight. It snaps twice, testing your footing on the slick ice. You hold steady and strike back hard.
Cold seeps through every seam of your armor by now. The wolf seems immune to it, thick furred and fast. It lunges low, going for your legs instead. You jump the strike and counter down hard.

A thin crack of daylight breaks through the heavy clouds. The wolf's eyes catch it and flash briefly gold. It uses the distraction to lunge hard at your side. You barely twist clear of the bite in time.

The floe groans loudly under shifting weight nearby. Both of you freeze completely, listening to the ice settle. Then the wolf breaks first, charging straight ahead fast. You brace hard and meet it dead on.

Your blade rings loud against the wolf's raised foreleg. Sparks of cold light scatter across the dark ice. It yelps and pulls back, favoring the hurt limb. You press the advantage hard while you still can.

The wind shifts suddenly and carries the smell of blood. The wolf's nose twitches hard, drawn by the scent. It circles wider now, more cautious than before. You keep your guard up, waiting for the lunge.
Snow squeaks under your boots as you retreat one step. The wolf advances to match, unhurried and sure. It feints toward your shield, then goes low instead. You catch the real strike just in time.

The Winter Wolf shakes frost from its coat mid stride. Flecks of ice scatter across the frozen ground below. It uses the moment to close half the distance. You raise your blade fast to meet the charge.
The Young Remorhaz claws up through cracked floe ice, steam hissing off its segmented back. Frost lights its eyes. You brace on the rail as the deck tilts, boots skidding toward the dark water below.
Heat rolls off the worm in waves, melting the snow to slush around its coils. The wreck groans under its weight. You grip your weapon and count to three, waiting for the roar that always comes first.
It surfaces through a skylight of ice, glowing like a coal dropped in snow. The whole cabin goes warm and wrong. You duck the tail as it swings, plates of frozen wood splintering past your head.
Under the aurora the beast looks almost gentle, blue light sliding down its ember hide. Then it strikes, fast as a whip, and the calm shatters into cracking ice and your own ragged breath.
The remorhaz rears from a drift, snow sloughing off its back in a hissing curtain. Steam fogs your vision. You plant your feet on the tilted deck and ready the first swing of initiative.
It comes up through the hole in the hull, segmented and glowing, melting the rime on the bulkhead behind it. The furnace smell hits before the heat does. You raise your guard and hold.
Cracks race across the floe as the worm surges beneath it, hunting the warmth of your torch. The ice groans, then splits. You leap the gap just as its jaws close on empty, frozen air.
Its glow lights the whole wreck gold for a moment, ember scales hissing where they touch the frost. You feel the heat before you see it move. The fight starts with your own held breath.
The young remorhaz drags itself over the rail, hide steaming where snow melts against it. Rotten planks crack beneath its coils. You back toward the hatch, weapon up, counting your die.
Deep under the drift something glows, and the ice above it starts to weep meltwater. You realize too late it is already moving. The worm bursts free in a shower of broken floe.
It slams into the mast, segmented body coiling tight, furnace heat pressing at your face like an open oven door. You swing first, before it can, and the fight finally begins.
The beast rises through a gap in the frozen deck, glowing ember eyes fixed on your torch. Ice hisses to steam where it passes. You steady your grip and step forward into the heat.
A crack echoes down the hold as the remorhaz forces its bulk through splintering wood. Warm air rushes past you, wrong in this cold. You raise your blade as its head swings round.
Snow sloughs off in sheets as the worm heaves itself onto the tilted deck, hide glowing like banked coals. The whole wreck creaks under new weight. You brace, and the duel begins.
It comes fast across the ice, steam trailing behind like a torn sail, jaws already open. You dive past the rail as its coils slam the spot where you stood a breath before.
The remorhaz coils around the broken mast, melting frost to water that hisses on its back. You circle wide on the tilted deck, waiting for an opening before it strikes again.
Frozen planks buckle as the beast forces itself up from below, glowing hot in the dim wreck. The cold air fights the heat and loses. You lift your weapon, ready for its charge.
A furnace glow spills from the hull breach, painting the ice gold. Something segmented and hungry is climbing toward the deck. You set your feet and wait for the first strike.
The worm bursts from a drift near the bow, spraying shattered ice across the deck. Steam hisses off its back in the frozen air. You roll clear and come up swinging your blade.
It rears against the aurora, ember light flickering along its coils, and the whole frozen hull seems to hold its breath. Then it strikes, and your fight for the wreck begins.
Heat shimmers over the ice as the remorhaz drags itself from the hole in the hull. Snow melts to slush under its glow. You grip the rail, steady your nerve, and step in close.
The young worm slams against the bulkhead, segmented plates hissing steam into the cold air. Splinters rain from the ceiling. You duck low and drive your blade toward its side.
Cracks spread fast across the floe as the beast surges beneath, hunting warmth through the ice. You feel the deck shift under your boots just before the water opens wide.
It climbs from the wreck's flooded hold, glowing hotter with every coil that clears the water. Steam fogs the frozen air around it. You raise your guard against the coming charge.
The remorhaz uncoils along the tilted deck, melting frost wherever its hide brushes wood. The old ship groans in protest. You set your stance and wait for its first lunging strike.
Snow hisses to steam where the worm presses against the broken rail, hide glowing ember bright. The whole wreck feels suddenly warm. You step forward, weapon raised, and swing.
A shower of ice crystals falls as the beast smashes through the frozen skylight above. Its glow lights the whole cabin gold. You brace against the bulkhead and ready your attack.
The young remorhaz noses out from beneath a drift, steam curling off its segmented back. Its eyes find your torch first. You grip your weapon tighter as it starts to move.
Heat rolls across the deck in waves as the worm drags its bulk from the flooded hold. Frost melts and drips from every beam. You plant your feet and brace for its first lunge.
It slams through rotten planks near the bow, glowing hide hissing against the frozen wood. Splinters scatter across the ice. You raise your blade and step into its reach.
The remorhaz coils tight against the mast, ember light pulsing along its plated back. Snow melts around it in a slow ring. You circle for an opening before it strikes first.
Cracks spread beneath your boots as the beast surges under the floe, hunting your warmth. The ice groans loud enough to drown the wind. You leap clear just as it breaks through.
A furnace glow lights the wreck's hold as the worm forces itself up through the gap. Steam fogs the frozen air thick. You steady your grip and wait for the first strike to land.
The young remorhaz rears against the aurora's glow, hide steaming in the frozen night. The whole deck seems to hold its breath. You step forward, weapon raised, and the fight starts.
It bursts from the drift near the stern, spraying shattered ice in a wide hissing arc. Snow melts fast where it lands, hissing louder. You roll aside and come up ready to strike back hard.
Heat radiates off the beast as it climbs the tilted deck, segmented plates glowing dull ember. The old wood creaks under its weight. You brace against the rail and hold your ground.
The remorhaz slams against the bulkhead, steam hissing loud in the frozen quiet. Splinters of ice and wood scatter past your head. You duck low and drive forward with your blade.
Snow sloughs away as the worm uncoils from beneath the frozen hull, glowing ember hot. The whole wreck seems to sigh under new weight. You set your stance and wait for its charge.
A crack of ice announces the beast before it appears, surging up through the broken floe. Steam trails behind its segmented back. You brace your feet and ready your weapon.
It climbs through the skylight in a shower of frost, hide glowing gold against the dark. The cabin fills fast with furnace heat. You raise your guard and step toward its coils.
The young remorhaz noses along the tilted deck, melting frost with every slow step. Its glow lights the whole hold amber. You grip your weapon and wait for the first lunge.
Heat shimmers off the worm as it forces itself through the hull breach, plates hissing steam. The frozen air fights back and loses fast. You steady your nerve and step forward.
Cracks spread across the ice floe as the beast surges beneath, hunting the warmth of your torch. The deck tilts hard under your boots. You leap the widening gap just in time.
The remorhaz slams through rotten planks near the mast, glowing hide scattering splinters wide. Snow hisses to steam all around. You duck the tail and drive your blade in close.
A furnace glow spills up through the flooded hold as the worm climbs toward the deck. Steam fogs thick in the frozen air. You brace against the rail and ready your first strike.
The young worm rears from a drift near the bow, ember eyes fixed on your torch flame. Snow melts fast in a ring around it. You grip your weapon tight and step into its reach.
It bursts through the frozen skylight above, glowing hide hissing steam into the cold. Ice crystals shower the tilted deck around your boots. You roll clear and come up swinging hard.
Heat rolls across the hold as the remorhaz drags its coils from the flooded dark. Frost melts and drips from every beam around you. You plant your feet and brace for the charge.
The beast slams against the mast, segmented plates glowing dull ember in the gloom. The old ship groans and shudders hard. You circle wide, waiting for an opening to strike.
Snow sloughs off in sheets as the worm heaves itself up through the broken hull. Steam curls thick around its glowing hide. You steady your grip and wait for its first lunge.
Cracks race fast beneath the floe as the beast surges toward the warmth of your torch. The ice groans and starts to split. You leap clear just as the water opens wide below.
The young remorhaz noses out from the flooded hold, glowing ember bright in the dark. Steam fogs the frozen air around it thick. You raise your blade and step forward, ready.

The frost giant swings its greataxe in a slow, heavy arc, and you drop below the blade just in time. Ice chips spray past your face like tiny knives. The blow carves a gash in the floe behind you, pale blue and steaming.

Forty feet tall and hung with icicles, it plants both boots and the whole floor shudders under the sudden weight. You feel the crack race under your knees before you even hear it snap through the ancient, groaning ice sheet.

It hurls a chunk of the frozen wreck at you like a toy, and you tiptoe past the falling wreckage, breath fogging in ragged gasps. The chunk lands where you stood a moment before, hissing steam into the frictionless white snow.

The giant leans in close, its breath a wall of frost that stings your throat and freezes your eyelashes solid. You roll under its reaching arm, watching thick tendons bulge pale along its massive, scarred wrist.

A greataxe swing goes wide and bites the aurora lit ice instead of you. The giant roars in frustration, and the sound rolls down a cavern that seems to swallow every other noise whole, leaving only silence.
You dodge left as a massive fist crashes down where you stood a heartbeat before. The ice sheet buckles beneath the blow, a spiderweb of cracks racing outward under its huge, cracked knuckles.

Snowflakes hang frozen in midair for a moment before the giant's swing sends them scattering wide across the chamber. Its bruised, cracked knuckles look almost human, just twenty times the size you expected them to be.
It hoists half a frozen rowboat and swings it like a club at your head. You slide flat across the slick floe, chin scraping ice, dagger up and ready for whatever comes next.

The frost giant's crit lands square on your shield arm, and pain shoots straight to your elbow and shoulder. You grit your teeth, roll clear, and answer with a strike of your own before it can recover its balance.
Cold radiates off its skin like an open icebox, fogging your vision white and numbing your fingers to the bone. You use the moment to close in low and drive your blade behind its exposed, bent knee.
A cliff of blue ice looms behind the giant as it charges forward across the chamber. You feint right before ducking under its reach, boots skidding wildly on the frictionless stone floor beneath you.
It bellows and the whole cavern answers back with a hundred cracks, ice shelves groaning under the sheer weight of forty tons of muscle and cold, ancient fury. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.

You watch its ornamental armor, some looted breastplate three sizes too small, crack loudly along the seam as it twists its huge bulk to swing again at you. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.
The greataxe drags a deep groove through the ice as it passes, close enough that you feel the wind of it lift your hair before you finally find an opening to counter. Your lungs burn in the cold air.

A snowdrift bursts apart as the giant stomps down hard, and shards of packed snow sting your cheek while you scramble low for an opening near its exposed ankle. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.
It grabs a hanging icicle the length of a spear and hurls it hard across the chamber. You drop flat, feeling it whistle just past your ear and shatter somewhere in the dark behind you.

The whole chamber seems to hold its breath as the giant lifts its axe overhead, aurora light rippling green and gold across the deep, cold blue of the blade. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.

You use the frictionless slope to your advantage, sliding low under a swing that would have taken your head clean off at the neck in an instant. Far off, another slab of ice calves loose with a crash.
Ice cracks beneath your knees as you plant hard for a strike, and the giant's counter nearly knocks you sideways into the black water below the shifting floe. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.

A gust off its swing scatters loose snow like smoke, and for a second you lose sight of it entirely before the shadow lunges back out of the swirling white. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.
The giant knocks aside your guard with contemptuous ease, and you tumble back hard across bruised knees, scrambling fast to find your footing again on the ice. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.
It reaches for you with fingers like frozen, cracked logs, and you slip past at the last instant, driving your blade deep into the meat of its exposed forearm. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.

Somewhere below your boots, the ice groans like it might give way entirely, and the giant ignores it completely, intent only on crushing you flat against the snow. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

You feint toward its knee and it drops its guard low in response, and that half second of hesitation is all you need to land a solid, glowing ray of damage. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.
The frost giant howls when your blade finally finds the gap in its armor, and the sound rattles loose icicles from the cavern roof high above your head. Steam curls up from the fresh gash in the ice.

Its greataxe cleaves through the space where you stood a breath ago, and the backswing catches a hanging icicle, shattering it into a hundred glittering knives of ice. Your lungs burn in the cold air.
You brace against the freezing wind pouring off its body, teeth chattering hard, and force yourself forward for one more desperate strike at its exposed ribs. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.
The giant plants its axe in the ice like a flag and roars, daring you closer, and you circle wide instead, watching carefully for any opening at all in its guard. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

A crack spreads fast under your boots as you dodge, and cold black water wells up through the seam, forcing you back toward drier, safer footing nearby on the floe. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

It swings low this time, sweeping hard for your legs, and you leap the blade by a hair, landing rough on numb, bruised knees against the frozen ice below. Steam curls up from the fresh gash in the ice.
The giant's fist slams down on the floe and sends a shockwave rolling through the ice that nearly drops you flat on your face in the packed snow. Somewhere above, the aurora flickers on, indifferent.

You catch a glint of firelight from somewhere deep in the wreck, just enough light to see the giant's next swing coming and dodge clear of it in time. A shard of ice grazes your cheek, sharp and cold.

It drags its axe behind it like a plow, carving a long pale scar in the blue ice, and you use the grinding noise to mask your quiet approach along the wall. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.

The cold bites hard at your fingers even through thick gloves, and you flex them once before gripping your weapon tight for another long exchange of heavy blows. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.

A shard of the frozen wreck's hull breaks free and the giant kicks it at you like a boulder, forcing a desperate roll aside into the deep, packed snow. A shard of ice grazes your cheek, sharp and cold.

You hear its knees crack like breaking floes as it shifts its enormous weight, readying another overhead swing clearly meant to end the fight right there and then. The cold seeps deep into your bones.
The giant smiles, an ugly stretch of frost cracked lips pulling wide across its face, and swings its greataxe in a full circle meant to catch you no matter where you stand.
Wind howls through the wreck's broken hull, and the giant times its swing to the gust, hoping the noise of the storm will cover its heavy approach across the ice. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

You slide under its reach on scraped knees, blade held up high, and open a shallow cut along its shin before rolling clear again into the open chamber. A shard of ice grazes your cheek, sharp and cold.

The floe cracks louder with every stomp it takes, and you realize the real danger might be the ice giving way entirely before the giant even lands a hit. Steam curls up from the fresh gash in the ice.

It hurls a spear of solid ice torn straight from the cavern wall, and you barely twist aside as it buries itself deep in the snow where your chest just was. The whole cavern seems to lean in and wait.

The giant's roar sends loose snow sifting from the cavern roof like slow, silent rain, and you use the cover of it to slip quietly toward its blind side. Steam curls up from the fresh gash in the ice.

You watch thick tendons ripple along its massive arm as it winds up for another blow, and you already know which way to dodge before the axe even starts to fall. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.

A pale light flickers off the aurora above as the giant advances slowly, its breath frosting the air between you into a solid, drifting wall of white mist. The wreck groans low in the dark behind you.
It stomps twice, feinting hard toward your left, and you almost fall for it before diving clear of the real swing that follows a heartbeat later through the cold air. Your lungs burn in the cold air.

The giant grips the greataxe with both massive hands now, done toying with you entirely, and the whole cavern seems to lean in close and watch in tense silence. Your breath fogs thick in front of you.
You feel the cold seep slowly through your boots as you circle wide around it, watching the giant's knees closely for the telltale shift before it finally lunges. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

A crack of ice like distant thunder announces the giant's next stomp, and you use the sudden sound to time your dash toward its exposed, open flank. Your grip tightens as the silence presses in close.
It swings the greataxe backhand, faster than something that size should ever be able to move, and you barely get your guard up in time to block the crushing blow. The cold seeps deep into your bones.

The wreck's frozen ropes creak loudly overhead as the giant brushes past them, and loose chunks of ice rain down steadily onto the ground between you both. The wreck groans low in the dark behind you.
You duck a swing meant clearly for your skull and answer low, driving cold steel deep into the soft joint behind its bruised, frostbitten knee. Far off, another slab of ice calves loose with a crash.

The giant staggers back a step, more surprised than truly hurt, and you press the advantage hard before the shifting, cracking ice beneath you both can move again. The cold seeps deep into your bones.
The wind eats every sound but your own breathing. Ahead, a white hummock shifts, rises, becomes a shoulder twenty feet tall. It turns. Two black eyes open in the fur, and the cold gets colder still.

Ice chips spray as something huge rolls over behind the frozen wreck. You catch a flash of matted white fur, a mouth wide enough to swallow a shield whole. The abominable yeti has found you first, and it is not hungry for retreat.

A hummock of snow was never a hummock at all. It stands, spine long, shoulders grown wide as a door, and the whiteout swallows the roar it lets loose thirty feet away, rattling ice off the wrecked hull nearby.

The aurora flickers green over the ice as a shape detaches itself from a drift near the wreck. Frost cracks off its fur as it rises. You realize, too late, you have been standing in its hunting ground the whole time.

Something pale moves past the wrecked hull, slow and huge, dragging a trail of packed snow behind it. It is not snow drifting on the wind. It is thirteen feet of muscle and claw, deciding whether you are worth the chase.

The silence breaks first, a low breathing sound rolling out from behind a ridge of packed ice. Then the ridge stands up, shoulders wide as the broken mast beside it, and the yeti fixes you with eyes like chips of black glass.

You almost walked past it entirely, boots crunching on old ice near the wreck. The frozen wreck's shadow moved, and moving shadows out here are never good news, not with claws this long and a chest that heaves like a bellows.

A gust flings ice needles into your face, and through the whiteout a shape resolves: white fur, hunched back, arms longer than a door is tall. The abominable yeti has been watching you approach for longer than you would like.

Cracks race across the floe underfoot as something heavy shifts its weight nearby, unseen in the blowing snow. You turn slow, breath fogging. Twenty feet away, a mountain of fur exhales frost and takes one enormous step.

The cave mouth breathes cold air like a throat, steady and low. Something inside answers with a growl, and a shape unfolds from the dark, matted fur catching the last grey light of the arctic afternoon as it steps into view.

Snow sweeps sideways across the ice shelf in ragged curtains, and for a moment you think the shape ahead is only a drift settling in the wind. Then it turns its head slowly, and the drift, it turns out, has eyes.

Your boots crunch on old ice near the wrecked hull, careful and quiet. Behind a curl of frozen rope, something huge is curled too, breathing slow and even, until your shadow crosses its face and the breathing stops cold.

A comet of ice dust trails the shape as it drops from a ledge above without a sound of warning, thirteen feet of white fur landing hard enough to crack the floe beneath your boots and send frost skittering everywhere.

The wreck's broken mast still stands against the wind, frost thick on every splintered plank. Something taller than the mast itself steps out slowly from behind it, unhurried, and the wind seems to hold its breath along with you.
Out past the last hummock the world is only white in every direction, until a shoulder the size of a boulder rises from the drift and turns toward the crunch of your approaching footsteps.

A low rumble rolls under the ice like distant thunder, felt more than heard through your boots. It is not thunder at all. It is a chest twenty feet away, rising and falling, deciding whether tonight is the night it charges.

The gallery of ice pillars glows faintly with borrowed aurora light, green and cold. Between two of the tallest a shape waits, patient as the ice itself, fur bright even in the dim shifting light overhead.

You hear the crack of a floe splitting apart before you ever see why. The abominable yeti has planted one huge foot on the ice, and the whole frozen shelf groans and answers it with a spray of white powder.

Wind howls through the wrecked ship's broken ribs like a flute carved from bone. Something answers the sound from deep inside, low and slow, and steps out into the light, matted white fur steaming in the cold.
The hunger in its eyes is older than the wreck rusting quietly around it. Thirty feet of frozen silence stretch between you, then one slow step, ice crunching under a paw the size of a full shield.

A shudder runs through the ice shelf as something massive shifts its stance somewhere nearby, unseen. You freeze mid step, breath fogging thick, and watch a white shoulder rise slowly past a low ridge of snow.
Snow curls off a drift that should not be moving on a still day, and then it is not a drift at all but a huge white shape rising to its full height, black eyes finding you at once across the ice.

The frozen wreck creaks under a gust, its hull leaning further into the drift, and past it something bigger than the ship itself uncurls slowly from behind the broken keel, shaking frost from matted fur.
Out of the whiteout comes a low breathing, steady as a bellows working a forge, and then two black eyes catch what little light the aurora offers through the blowing snow overhead.

Ice needles sting your face as the wind picks up without warning, and through the sting you see it clearly: fur white as the storm, shoulders wide as a door, closing the distance one long stride at a time.
A shape the size of a hummock stands slowly from the snow near the wrecked hull, shaking loose frost from its fur in a shower, and the whole floe seems to lean under its sudden shifting weight.
The cracking of the ice comes first, sharp and sudden, then the shape that made it, thirteen feet tall and pale as bone, stepping free of a drift with claws already flexing at its sides.
Silence eats the sound of your own careful steps, until a low growl fills the gap all at once, rolling out from behind a curtain of hanging ice near the mouth of a shallow cave.
The aurora paints the ice in green and violet ribbons, and under it a huge shape rises slowly from behind a hummock, fur catching the shifting color like frost catching a distant fire.
You spot the tracks first, huge and deep, pressed fresh into the snow near the wreck's broken hull, and then you spot what made them, watching you patiently from twenty feet off.
A gust tears the whiteout open for one long heartbeat, just long enough to show fur, claws, and a mouth wide enough to end the fight before it ever really starts.
The wrecked hull groans as something braces its full weight against it from the far side, and then the something steps calmly around, tall as the broken mast standing beside it.
Cold radiates off the ice in visible waves, but a colder chill entirely comes from the shape rising slowly behind a snowbank, its breath steaming out in great slow clouds.
A low rumble shakes loose snow from the wreck's frozen rigging as the abominable yeti steps fully into view, eyes fixed on you like it has been waiting patiently all along for this.
The floe cracks under sudden weight just behind you, and turning fast shows a wall of white fur closing in, shoulders hunched low, claws spread wide and ready to strike.
Past the last ridge of packed snow the world opens into a shallow bowl of ice, and standing dead center of it is something far too large to be anything but serious trouble.
The wind drops for one strange moment, and in that sudden stillness you hear breathing that is not your own, slow and huge, coming steadily from behind the frozen wreck.
Frost sparkles off a shape near the cave mouth, still as stone, and then the shape moves, unfolding slowly to its full height, claws raking long grooves into the packed ice.
You catch the shine of two eyes buried in a drift ahead, and a heartbeat later the whole drift rises up, towering over you, shaking snow from a coat thick as any armor.
The wreck's broken deck groans under something heavy pacing slowly above it, and then the pacing stops cold, and a huge white head leans over the rail to look down at you.
Out on the ice shelf a low howl rolls across the whiteout, closer this time than the last one, and now you can finally see the shape that is making it.
A hummock near the wreck breathes out a slow cloud of frost, and you realize with a chill that hummocks do not breathe, not unless something huge is curled up tight inside.
The aurora dims as a long shadow passes beneath it, cast by something rising from behind a low ridge, shoulders broad enough to blot out the ship's entire broken mast.
Snow sprays wide as claws the length of daggers rake hard across the ice, and the abominable yeti straightens slowly to its full towering height, eyes locking onto yours.
The silence out here has real weight, and it breaks all at once when a shape twenty feet tall steps out from behind the frozen wreck, fur bristling thick with frost.
You feel the ground shudder before you even hear it, huge footsteps closing steadily from behind a curtain of blowing snow, slow, patient, and completely deliberate.
A frozen rope creaks sharply near the wreck's stern, pulled taut by something huge leaning its full weight against the hull, watching you closely through the whiteout.
The cave behind the ice falls utterly silent, then exhales one long breath of cold air, and something pale steps calmly out to meet the sound of your crunching boots.
Past the cracked floe a mound of snow rises like it has been waiting patiently for you, shaking loose in a sudden shower of frost to reveal fur, claws, and fangs.
The wind carries a low growl clean across the ice shelf, and moments later a huge white shape crests a nearby ridge, claws already flexing slowly at its sides.
A shard of ice snaps free from the wreck's rail and clatters down hard, and the sound draws a huge shape up from the dark below deck, climbing slowly to face you.
The whiteout thins just enough to show a shape hunched low near a drift, and then it rises, taller than the wrecked mast beside it, breath steaming thick in the cold air.
11: The wreck's frozen hull groans under fresh snow as the white dragon uncoils from its icy hoard, frost rolling off its wings in sheets and settling on broken beams. You plant your boots on the cracking ice, breath fogging thick, and wait for the first blast of cold before the real fight begins.

It rears against the cracking floe, wings unfurling wide enough to blot out the aurora overhead, and the cold hits like a wall, marrow deep and merciless. For a long moment nobody moves, then it roars and the whole frozen world seems to shake loose beneath your boots at once.

Ice shards spray from its jaws before the breath even lands, a warning shot across the frozen wreck that rattles loose snow from every broken beam and rusted plate. You hold your line, shield raised, waiting to see if your armor is worth the coin you spent buying it.

The dragon's tail cracks the floe behind you like a whip, sending a spiderweb of fractures racing toward your boots through the drifting snow and old wreckage. You dodge sideways, blade ready, as its pale eyes track your every step across the cracking, groaning ice.

Silence swallows the fight the instant the wind dies, leaving only your own heartbeat and the slow drag of claws on ice somewhere close behind you in the fog. Then the white dragon exhales, and the whole world turns to a hissing cloud of frost and flying shards.

You duck under a swipe of its wing and feel the frozen air needle straight through your coat, cold enough to steal your breath entirely. The dragon coils tighter around its hoard, daring you closer, daring you to make the first real move of this long fight.

Cracks spread wide under the dragon's weight as it climbs onto the highest shelf of shattered ice, wings spread for a dive that will surely crush anything unlucky enough to stand beneath it. You brace, knowing the coming crash will test both your footing and your nerve.

The cold bites harder than any blade, exposed skin burning wherever the wind finds a seam in your gear and worms its way through. Still you press forward, watching the dragon's chest rise before another icy blast, timing your guard to the instant its breath fills.

A low rumble rolls across the frozen wreck before the dragon even moves, ice groaning under its bulk like a warning nobody standing here wants to hear twice. You grip your weapon tighter, feeling the fight's opening seconds stretch impossibly long and cold.

The white dragon skids across a frictionless patch of ice, claws scrabbling for purchase, and for one perfect instant it is fully exposed to attack. You take the shot without hesitation, blade biting deep before it can recover its footing on the treacherous frozen floe.

Wind screams through the wreck's broken ribs as the dragon circles overhead, wings throwing long shadows across the cracking ice below your boots. You watch its tail instead of its jaws, knowing full well the real danger rides in that lash more than anything else.

The dragon's roar sends a shiver down every crack in the ice, and somewhere below the floe answers back with a low groan of its own making. You steady your stance, aware the ground itself might turn against you before this long, brutal fight ever truly ends.

Frost creeps up your blade the moment you raise it, the dragon's mere presence enough to chill the steel clean through your gloves. You exhale slowly, watching its wings tense, and time your charge to the breath right before it unleashes the freezing cone.

The wreck's frozen hull becomes your only real cover as icy shards rain sideways across the whole battlefield without any warning at all. You press your back to it, waiting for a gap in the dragon's rhythm to close the distance fast and strike true.

It lands hard enough to crack a fresh line clean across the floe, claws digging desperately for purchase on the slick, treacherous surface. You seize the opening at once, sprinting low across the ice before the dragon can turn its whole bulk back toward you.

The aurora flickers overhead, cold light glinting off scales the color of old undisturbed snow drifted deep against the wreck. For a moment the dragon almost looks peaceful, coiled and still, before its head snaps toward you and the fight truly begins in earnest.

A wounded shriek rattles the frozen wreck as your blade finds a seam between two great overlapping plates of pale scale. The dragon thrashes wildly, sending shards of ice skittering everywhere across the frozen floe, and you brace hard for its coming retaliation.

You feel the cold before you even see the breath coming, a pressure in your ears like the whole sky dropping all at once. Then the white dragon opens its jaws wide and the world turns to a hissing, freezing blur of frost and screaming wind and ice.

The floe cracks beneath both of you as the dragon lunges hard, claws raking toward your exposed flank without any mercy at all. You roll clear just in time, ice spraying past your shoulder, and come up already swinging for its undefended side.

Its wings beat once, twice, throwing loose snow into a stinging cloud that hides its next move completely from your view. You listen instead of watching, tracking the drag of claws on ice carefully to guess exactly where the next strike will land.

The dragon settles low over its hoard, guarding gold and bone alike, pale eyes never once leaving you as you circle warily around it. Every step you take grinds ice loudly beneath your boots, loud in a silence that otherwise eats every single sound whole.

A crack splits the air like a whip the instant the ice itself gives way, and you feel the whole floe tilt sharply beneath your boots. The dragon rides the sudden shift easily, wings spread wide for balance, while you scramble hard to keep your footing.

Frost blooms across your blade in an instant, drawn out by the dragon's cold presence even before it fully attacks you. You grip tighter, breath fogging thick in the freezing air, and watch its chest for the telltale rise before the next icy blast.

The white dragon claws up a drift of loose snow, sending it sheeting sideways in a blinding wall between the two of you both. You press forward through it anyway, blade first, trusting your ears far more than your eyes in the sudden whiteout.

Its tail slams the ice with a sound like thunder cracking underwater, and the whole frozen wreck seems to ring with the force of impact. You keep low, waiting for the tremor to pass completely before closing the gap and striking hard at its flank.

A low groan from deep in the ice warns you the floe beneath the dragon is close to giving way entirely and without warning. You circle wide, hoping the ground itself does some of your work before this long fight grinds you down instead.

The dragon rears back on its haunches, wings thrown wide against the shimmering aurora, and for one heartbeat it looks almost beautiful and calm. Then its jaws part and the beauty turns instantly into a screaming wall of frost and sudden fury.

Snow explodes upward where its claws strike hard, burying your boots to the ankle in a single freezing instant of cold. You wrench yourself free, cold biting through every seam of your armor, and press the attack before it resets its stance again.

The wreck groans around you both like it remembers older battles fought long ago across this same frozen ground and ice. You plant your feet firmly, watching the dragon's coiled muscle, and wait for the opening its pride will eventually give you.

Ice needles sting your cheeks as the wind picks up hard, carrying the dragon's low growl across the cracking floe toward you fast. You narrow your eyes against the glare and start closing the distance before that low growl becomes a full roar.

The dragon's wing catches an updraft and for a moment it hangs above the wreck like a pale cloud grown sudden claws and teeth. You use the lull to reposition, boots crunching softly on the ice, before it drops back down hard into the fight.

A shard of ice the size of your whole arm skitters past your head, thrown loose by the dragon's thrashing tail in fury. You duck low, feel the cold wind of its passing brush your neck, and press forward fast before it winds up to swing again.

9: Glowcap spores drift like slow snow: breathe deep (just once) and the tunnel spins!
Can you hear it? A tentacle (barbed, patient) tests the dark for your footsteps.
Roll for it: the grick ancient lunges 2d6+3 and the gallery floor cracks wide!
Ichor drips off the beak - black, cold, and thick (like tar left in a cave).
DC 15 to keep your footing: the shelf of rock tilts & the chasm yawns below.
Ten teeth, one bite: does the beak (curved like a hook) find flesh or stone?
Torches flare & gutter as the tentacle rears - thirty feet of hungry muscle!
Nonmagical bludgeoning barely dents it: only a sharp edge (or fire) truly hurts.
Nine damage from a single lash? The grick ancient hits harder than it should!
Grooves in the wall (old scars, deep ones) mark where claws raked before you.
Resistance to piercing means daggers barely bite: try force instead (if you have it)!
Bottleneck ahead: squeeze through or fight here, where the ceiling presses down?
A blade finds the gap between plates - 1d8+2 damage, and the beast recoils!
Fungal glow paints the cavern green & sick, just enough light to see the teeth.
Regenerate? No - this thing doesn't heal fast, but it sure doesn't quit either!
Sixteen feet of tentacle uncoils from the ceiling (you never saw it hanging there).
Weather the first swing, then strike back: DC 13 or lose your footing entirely!
Worm-like body flexes around the pillar - where does the head even begin?
Chin-deep in spores now: cough twice (roll a d4) or lose your next turn!
Barrel roll under the lash & come up swinging - risky, but it might just work!
Grick ancient rears back, tentacle high, and asks (in its own way): run or fight?
Five points of damage, sided steel biting deep - and still it barely slows!
Radiant torchlight scorches the beak (40% less damage from cold steel, sadly).
Doubled over the ridge, it strikes low & fast: dodge or eat six points of pain!
Stone drips ichor where the last fight happened (nobody wants a repeat of that)!
Left wall, right wall, close in: the bottleneck traps you both (choose fast)!
Hide behind the pillar? Or step into the glow and swing for 2d4+1 instead?
A punch of force magic (rare down here) cracks the beak clean in half!
Four torches, one goes out - does the dark make the tentacle bolder now?
Loud crack of stone: the shelf gives way & the fight tumbles to a lower ledge!
Ten feet away, the beak snaps shut on empty air (lucky - this time).
Barbed hide shrugs off nonmagical hits: cold iron or silver, please & thank you!
Cavern walls glow faint gray - enough to aim, not enough to feel safe (ever).
Six damage lands true: the grick ancient shrieks & the cavern shakes with it!
Slip on wet stone (DC 12) or plant your feet and drive the blade home!
Threads of drowned moss hang from the ceiling - do they hide more teeth?
Opened wound along its flank: does it flee now, or fight twice as hard?
Point-blank range: the tentacle can't miss, but neither, this time, can you!
First branch of the cavern splits three ways: which one hums with danger?
Punch through its guard (DC 14) and land 1d10+1 before it recoils hard!
Bludgeoning barely scratches the hide - switch weapons, and switch fast!
Angry roar shakes loose dust from the ceiling (and your nerve, just a little).
Big and cold, the tentacle coils twice around a stalagmite before it strikes!
Round two: the beast rears again, tentacle high, patience clearly gone now.
Feet slip on the ridge (DC 11) - grab the wall or tumble toward the chasm!
Every glowcap nearby pulses once, as if warning: something huge just moved.
Hit it where the plates gap (sneak attack, +2d6) before it turns to face you!
Fine mist of spores settles on your shoulders: shake it off before it settles in!
Rock shelf uncoils dust as the tentacle rakes stone just inches from your boot!
Sixteen points of damage in one exchange - the fight tips, finally, your way!
Wet stone, dry stone, it doesn't matter: the beak strikes true either way!
Close the gap fast (move + attack) before the tentacle coils for another swing!
Gray ridge above hides a second grick? Better hope that torch keeps burning!
Last stand at the bottleneck: three feet wide, one beast, and nowhere to run!
Wait - did that fold of stone just breathe? (DC 15 to notice before it strikes!)
Cloaker attack: 2d6+4 slashing, plus a moan that saps courage (fear save DC 13)?
Engulfed! You're blind & restrained until you break free (DC 14 Strength check).
Ceiling ripples: is it rock, or a cloaker waiting to drop (60% chance it's both)?
Its bite lands for 3d6+2 - not bad for something shaped like a bedsheet!
Roll initiative: the cloaker gets +2 (surprise) & you get nothing but nerves.
Tail-wrap grapple check succeeds on a 12+? Then the real trouble begins!
Moan effect: everyone within 30 feet rolls a Wisdom save (DC 13) or flees!
You're at 40% HP & still swinging - can the next hit (1d8+3) finish it?
Cracks in the ceiling widen: cloaker drop attack incoming (advantage granted)!
Bite + engulf combo: 4d6 damage if both connect - do you dodge in time?
Disadvantage on your attack while wrapped: does the darkness ever let up?
Cloaker's underside teeth flash for 2d8+1 - who forgot to watch the roof?
Half its body billows like cloth; the other half hides a killing bite (surprise!).
DC 16 save or be dragged into the chasm - grab something, quick!
It misses (rolled a 4) & the whole party exhales at once!
A firebolt (2d10) scorches the thing mid-glide - finally, some light!
Ability check to shrug off fear: DC 13, and failure means you run.
Cloaker HP sits at 40% - one more solid hit (2d6+3) could end this!
Engulf saves cut damage in half if you resist (Con save DC 14)?
Tucked in a fold of stone: is it a boulder, or 60% likely something worse?
Its moan echoes down the passage - does fear (DC 13) beat resolve tonight?
You land a crit! Damage doubles: 4d6+4 instead of the usual 2d6+2.
Grapple broken (Str check beat DC 14) - now it's your turn to strike back!
Shadow detaches from the ceiling: initiative rolled, and it's not in your favor!
Bite for 3d6, then a wrap attempt - can you dodge the follow-up (AC 15)?
That 'wall' shivers & unfolds into teeth - run, or roll to attack (+5)?
Underdark air goes cold: cloaker's fear aura (30 ft, DC 13) locks your knees.
Its attack roll: a 19! Only a shield spell saves you from 2d8+3 damage.
Wrapped tight, you can't see - but you can still stab blind (disadvantage, -5)!
40% of its hit points gone already - keep the pressure on (2d6+2 per hit)!
Cloaker slips free of the net (DC 12 failed) & the chase begins again.
Sneak attack lands for 3d6+2 while it's distracted swallowing someone's shout!
Half-glimpsed shape on the cavern roof: cloaker (70%) or just plain shadow (30%)?
Its bite connects at 2d6+3, but the real threat is the engulf that follows!
You resist the moan (saved vs DC 13) - now go finish what it started!
Cracked stalactite drops as cover: does it block the cloaker's dive attack?
Wrapped victim takes 1d6 per round until freed (DC 14 Strength, repeatable)!
That 'cairn' twitches: is it settling stone, or a cloaker readying its bite?
Damage roll: 2d8+2 slashing - enough to make it loosen its grip at last!
Fear save failed (rolled a 6) - you're frozen while its teeth close in!
Engulfed target can't breathe: track rounds carefully (DC 14 each turn to escape)!
It billows overhead like a torn sail - then drops for 3d6+1 damage!
Crit threat range widened to 19-20? Then every swing here matters more.
You shove it off (opposed Athletics, DC 13) & scramble back from the fold.
Half its shape unfurls from the ceiling - the other half stays hidden (why)?
Attack roll of 17 clears its AC 15 - the blade finally finds true purchase!
Fleeing partygoers (fear DC 13 failed) leave you facing the cloaker alone!
That damp 'curtain' on the wall shivers: cloaker (DC 15 to spot) or draft?
One more failed save & you're wrapped tight for 1d4 rounds straight!
Firebolt (2d10+1) catches its edge as it billows - does it even flinch?
Grapple escape: roll a 14+ or stay engulfed for another brutal round!
Its moan hits for 0 damage but drops your morale (DC 13, disadvantage next turn)!
Cloaker's bite (2d6+3) plus wrap attempt: a nasty one-two in the dark.
You strike true (nat 20!) & the thing finally peels back off the wall.
Beard smoking, the fire giant swings its hammer: brace (or dodge) before it lands!
Can you feel that heat? Roll 2d6+4 and hope the anvil-sized fist misses (barely)!
Molten cracks spiderweb the cave floor - jump now, or take 1d8+2 burn damage!
Its warhammer arcs down at DC 15: block, dive, or eat a hit worth 3d6+1?
Steam hisses off its shoulders (40% chance the next swing is a critical one)!
Ready your blade - the giant roars, and torches gutter to almost nothing!
Behind the chained portcullis: something huge stirs, and it smells of ash & iron!
A boulder-sized fist crashes down (missed you by inches!) - counter now?
Do you duck, or trust your shield to eat 2d10+3 from that burning club?
Sparks rain from the anvil-forge as the giant bellows: run, or fight back?
Its beard catches fire (again!) and it doesn't even seem to notice the pain?
Kneel behind the rubble - the hammer's shockwave rolls out at DC 14 or worse!
Twelve feet of muscle & rage block the tunnel: what's your next move?
Molten light floods the chamber - can your eyes adjust in time (or not)?
A slashing blow finds the gap in your guard: take 1d10+3, no save allowed!
Its grip tightens on the haft (knuckles glowing red-hot) - brace yourself!
Fifty percent of that ceiling just came down - did you dodge in time?
Ash swirls thick as the giant stomps closer: 3 rounds until it reaches you!
Reflected firelight throws its shadow twenty feet tall - unnerved yet?
Quick! Roll DC 16 to keep your footing on the scorched, slick stone!
A missile of molten rock streaks past (inches from your skull!) - move!
Its hammer buries itself in stone where you stood - lucky, or fast?
Heat rolls off in waves: disadvantage on your next roll unless you retreat!
Grinning through soot & scars, it hefts the hammer for one more swing!
The chain around its wrist snaps taut & drags a boulder behind it!
Ember trails mark every swing - can you track the pattern (DC 13)?
A guttural roar shakes loose stone from the ceiling: cover, now!
Its knee buckles for just a moment - is that an opening, or a trap?
Sixteen points of damage waiting in that swing: block, or gamble on a dodge?
Molten drips sizzle on the floor between you (don't step there!) - move around!
A thunderous stomp cracks the bridge: do you risk the crossing anyway?
Its free hand grabs at you - roll DC 15 to slip the grasp!
Flames lick up its forearm (it barely flinches) - what does that tell you?
Coins & scorched gear litter the ground where past adventurers stood!
A wall of heat rolls forward: 20% chance you're staggered this round!
Its hammer's head glows white-hot - one touch and that's 2d6 fire damage!
Grunting, it hoists the hammer overhead (this is the big one - dodge!)!
Sparks fly as blade meets molten steel: does your weapon hold (DC 12)?
A crevice opens underfoot - jump, or fall into the dark below?
It bellows a challenge that rattles your teeth: answer with steel!
Smoke pools along the ceiling (visibility drops - disadvantage on ranged attacks)!
One more hit like that (3d8+2) and you're on the ropes - retreat?
Its beard flares gold in the firelight: is that pride, or just heat?
A rain of sparks forces you back - do you hold ground or fall behind cover?
Cracks race up the pillar it just struck: get clear before it topples!
Its hammer drags a groove through solid rock - respect that strength!
Between swings it staggers (a real opening!) - press the attack now?
Boiling steam vents from a crack in the floor: skirt around, or push through?
A glancing blow scores your shoulder: 1d6 damage, but you're still standing!
It plants both feet & winds up for a haymaker worth 4d6 if it connects!
