Your torch gutters in the heat - can you still see the next swing coming?
Rock shards fly from its last strike (DC 13 to avoid a stray hit)!
It roars again, louder: is that rage, or is it starting to worry?
One final glowing ember settles on your sleeve - stamp it out fast!
Hammer raised, eyes narrowed: this is the moment - strike, or wait?
Slime coats the ledge: one wrong step (and the dark takes you) means a 10-foot fall!
Roll for initiative: the aboleth's mind-probe hits DC 15 & your thoughts go slippery.
Tentacles unfurl from the black water (4 of them!) - do you dodge or hold your ground?
A lungful of stale air fills your lungs: hold it, or breathe deep & risk the spores?
The eel-thing's mucus does 2d6+3 damage on a hit - can your shield stop that much slime?
Columns of dripping stone hide something huge: do you light a torch, or stay blind?
Its bulging eyes track you in total dark: how does anything see without light?
Advantage on your next swing (if you dodge the tentacle first!) - worth the risk?
A chime rings deep in the cavern: is that a warning, or a trap set just for you?
The slime trail glows faintly: 40% chance it marks a nest nearby - follow it or flee?
Your blade finds the gap in its hide (2d8+2!) but the mucus dulls the edge fast.
Panic rises as the water churns: what waits below the surface, coiled & patient?
DC 17 to resist its enslaving gaze: fail, and you fight on its side for a round!
The cavern floor tilts toward a chasm: one shove and you're gone (no save, no shout).
Its tentacle wraps your ankle: escape needs a DC 13 check (+2 if you're greased).
Ancient columns lean at odd angles: were they built, or grown from something worse?
A whistle echoes off wet stone: is that the beast, or just trapped air escaping?
Roll a d20: on a 15+ you spot the slick patch before your boot finds it first!
The thing's mind reaches yours: block it, or let a single stray thought slip through?
Cold mucus drips from the ceiling (right onto your collar!) - do you flinch or fight?
Its whole body ripples like a drum skin: does that mean it's hurt, or just breathing?
You feel a hum in your chest: is that your heartbeat, or the aboleth's voice?
The slime pool bubbles once, twice, then goes still: what surfaces next (and how fast)?
A crit on this swing (19 or 20!) cracks its hide wide - do you press the advantage?
Deep water drowns all sound: can you hear the tentacle before it's already around you?
Its skin sheds a film that stings on contact: DC 12 CON save or take 1d4 extra!
The cave narrows to a crawl: do you squeeze through, or back off (and lose the lead)?
Something massive shifts in the black lake: ripples reach the shore before it does.
A stalactite cracks loose above (50% it lands on the beast, not you!) - worth waiting?
Its tentacle strikes for 2d6+1: block with your shield or dodge and hope for a miss?
The air tastes of brine down here: how far underground can a lake even reach?
You cup your hands to catch the drip: is it water, or something the aboleth left?
A single torch gutters low: do you press on in the dark, or fall back for oil?
Its call rings through the stone (a strange, sung note) & every fish nearby flees.
The ledge crumbles under one foot: DC 10 DEX save or slide toward the black water!
Bonus action: shove the creature back (if you beat its DC 14) before it grabs again.
Rubbery hide flinches at your strike: does that mean pain, or just a twitch of muscle?
The whole cavern seems to breathe with it: in, out, in - do you match its rhythm?
A stray tentacle brushes your shield (no damage, just cold slime) - do you strike now?
Its eyes fix on yours: DC 16 WIS save or stand frozen for a full round!
The dark hides more than the beast: what else moved back there, just out of torchlight?
You slip on the slick stone (DC 11 or fall prone!) right as the tentacle lunges.
A low groan echoes off wet columns: is the cavern settling, or is something else near?
Its mucus trail glows faint green: 30% chance it's toxic if your blade gets coated.
The lake's surface splits (four ripples at once!) - how many arms does this thing have?
You press your back to cold stone: safe for now, but for how long can that last?
A whistle of air through a crack sounds almost like words: do you listen, or run?
Its tentacle coils for another strike: dodge (DC 13) or brace and take the hit?
The chasm's edge crumbles more with every step: is there any safe way across?
Something ancient stirs below: does the water ripple from the current, or from it?
Your torch catches its eye (a flash of pale, wet skin!) before the dark swallows it.
A hero's stance won't save you here: only footwork, wit & a steady blade will.
The aboleth's roar (if that's even a roar) rattles loose stone from the ceiling!
One misstep near the lip of the lake ends the fight before it even starts: watch your feet!
10: The cavern narrows to a bottleneck where the ceiling drips slow and steady. A long shape uncoils, tentacle first. Your torch finds a ring of teeth. Six legs grip stone without a sound.
Grooves in the wet floor mark where something huge has crawled before. The air tastes of rot and cold stone. Ahead, a ridge glows faint green with fungal light. Whatever hunts here hunts by sound.
A stalagmite cracks under a heavy tread you cannot see. Glowcap spores puff into the dark, drifting like slow snow. Then teeth catch the light, ringed and hungry. You raise your blade and hold still.
The path splits at a ledge slick with old ichor. Something long lurches out of the black, moving faster than its size should allow. Six eyes catch your torch. There is no time to think, only swing.
Deep in the gallery the drip of water sounds like a clock. A tentacle uncoils along the shelf above, testing the air. You feel watched before you see the ring of teeth. It strikes without warning.

The grick ancient rears from a crack in the stone, gray and huge. Its hide looks like solid rock until it moves. Ichor steams where an old wound never healed. It hunts by the sound of your own breath.
Your footstool of loose rubble shifts and the whole ledge groans. Below, something coils tighter, patient as the dark itself. A tentacle lashes up before you can brace. The fight begins mid step.
Cold air pours from a crack wide enough for one body. Something ancient and gray fills it, teeth first. You hear it before torchlight finds it. Six legs anchor to stone like it has always lived here.

A rope of muscle uncoils from the ceiling, dropping without a sound. Its hide is armor, nonmagical and thick as bark. Ichor drips where an old blade once bit deep. You barely raise your rapier in time.

The bottleneck cavern smells of wet stone and older blood. Ridges in the rock hide a shape until it moves, gray tentacles first. It strikes fast, faster than something that size should be. You flinch back.

Somewhere past the chasm a rock cracks and skids loose. A shadow the size of a wagon shifts along the ridge. Its teeth ring an open mouth built to bite through bone. It hunts by the drum of your feet.

The path drops into a gallery ringed with old claw marks. A grick ancient uncoils from behind a fallen slab. Its tentacles taste the air before it lunges. Steaming ichor marks where it has bled before.
A soft drip becomes a drum becomes a roar. The cavern floor shakes as something huge shifts its coils. Torchlight catches a ring of teeth wide as a shield. There is no time left to retreat.

The tunnel narrows and the dark presses close on both sides. A tentacle brushes your shoulder before you even see it move. Six legs grip the ceiling like it weighs nothing. You swing before it can bite.
Deep grooves score the stone where something ancient has crawled for years. The air goes cold and still, waiting. Then a shape the color of wet slate rears up. Its teeth are the last thing you see.

A pauldron of loose stone clatters somewhere in the dark. Something long and coiling answers the sound at once. Its hide ignores the first glancing hit. Ichor beads where your blade finally finds skin.
The cave floor is slick with old spore dust and something worse. A ridge of stone shifts, revealing gray hide beneath. Tentacles lash out in a wide arc. You duck and feel the wind of the miss.

Somewhere ahead a torch gutters and dies against a sudden draft. In the black, six legs click against stone in patient rhythm. A ring of teeth opens wider than your shield. You strike where the sound was.
The chasm edge crumbles under careless footing. Below, something huge coils tighter around a fallen ladder. It rears with a roar that shakes loose gravel. Your rapier finds only hide at first.
A crack in the ceiling drips ichor, thick and dark. Something ancient waits above, patient as stone itself. It drops without warning, tentacles first. The fight is over before you catch your breath.
The bottleneck forces you shoulder to shoulder with the dark. A shape uncoils along the ridge, gray and huge. Its bite lands solid on your armor. Steaming ichor marks the answering strike.
Far back in the gallery a stalagmite topples with a crack. Something long and coiling answers at once, hunting the sound. Its teeth ring wide and hungry. You barely raise your blade in time.
The cavern floor is scarred with old claw grooves, deep and wide. A gray shape rears from behind a fallen slab. Tentacles test the air before it lunges. Its hide shrugs off your first hit.
Cold drips down the back of your neck as something shifts overhead. A ring of teeth catches the last of your torchlight. It drops fast, faster than its size should allow. You barely dodge the bite.
The path narrows past a ledge slick with dried ichor. Something ancient rears up, gray hide catching the glow. Its tentacles coil and lash without warning. You feel the strike before you see it land.
A low roar rolls through the chasm, shaking loose stone. Something huge shifts its coils along the ridge above. Six legs grip rock like it weighs nothing at all. Its bite comes fast and hard.
The gallery smells of old blood and wet fungus. A shape the size of a cart uncoils from the shadow. Its teeth ring wide, hunting by the sound of your breath. You brace and swing first.
Deep in the dark, something drags itself along a groove worn smooth by years. Torchlight finds a gray hide, thick as bark. Tentacles lash out before you can flinch. Ichor answers your blade.
The chasm narrows to a single crossing of loose stone. Something coils beneath it, patient and huge. It strikes the moment your foot lands. You barely catch your balance and swing back.
A crack of falling rock announces something ancient waking. Gray hide fills the tunnel mouth, tentacles testing the air. Its teeth ring wider than your shield. You hold your ground and strike.
The ridge above drips slow and steady, marking time. Something long uncoils, hunting the sound of your steps. Its bite lands hard on unblunted armor. Steaming ichor marks where you struck back.
Somewhere past the ladders a stone shelf groans and gives. Below, six legs grip the dark like it is home. A ring of teeth opens on a roar. You raise your blade against the coming lunge.
The cavern floor is slick with something worse than water. A gray shape rears from the ridge, ancient and huge. Its tentacles lash in a wide arc. You duck low and feel the miss pass overhead.
Cold air announces the crack before the shape does. Something huge shifts its coils, patient and quiet. Then it strikes, teeth ringed and hungry. Your rapier finds hide, hard and unyielding.
The path drops past a bottleneck thick with old spore dust. A shape uncoils from the shadow, gray and ancient. It hunts by the sound of dripping water. Its bite lands before your guard is up.
Deep grooves mark the stone where something huge has crawled for years, patient as the dark. It rears now, tentacles testing the air first. Teeth ring wide as a shield. You brace and strike back.

A stalagmite cracks loose somewhere in the black, and the sound draws an answer at once. Six legs click closer, hunting by ear alone. Its hide shrugs off the first glancing hit. Ichor beads at the second.

The chasm edge crumbles underfoot, loose gravel skidding into the dark. Below, something ancient coils tighter, roused by the noise. It lunges hard, tentacles first. You barely catch your footing and swing.

Somewhere in the gallery a torch gutters against a sudden cold draft. Six legs click patient rhythm against stone, closing in. A ring of teeth opens wide in the dark. You strike where the sound was loudest.
The cavern narrows past a ledge slick with dried ichor and older blood. A gray shape rears up, ancient and huge. Tentacles coil and lash without warning. Your blade meets hide, hard and unyielding.

A low roar shakes loose stone somewhere past the chasm, patient and huge. Six legs grip the ridge like it weighs nothing. Its bite comes fast, aimed at your unblunted armor. You brace and answer in kind.
The gallery smells of wet fungus and something worse underneath. A shape the size of a cart uncoils from shadow, hunting your breath. Its teeth ring wide and hungry. You swing first and feel it land.

Deep in the dark something drags itself along a groove worn smooth by years of patient hunting. Torchlight finds gray hide, thick as bark. Tentacles lash before you can flinch. Ichor answers your blade.
The chasm narrows to one crossing of loose, slick stone. Something huge coils beneath it, waiting on the sound of feet. It strikes the moment you land. You catch your balance and swing back hard.
A crack of falling rock wakes something ancient in the tunnel mouth. Gray hide fills the passage, tentacles testing the air. Its teeth ring wider than your shield. You hold ground and strike first.
The ridge above drips slow, marking time like a clock in the dark. Something long uncoils, hunting the sound of your steps. Its bite lands hard on your armor. Steaming ichor answers where you struck.
Somewhere past the ladders a stone shelf groans and finally gives way. Below, six legs grip the dark like it is home to them. A ring of teeth opens on a roar. You raise your blade against the lunge.
The floor is slick with something worse than water, dark and old. A gray shape rears from the ridge, ancient and patient. Its tentacles lash in a wide, sudden arc. You duck low and feel it pass over.
Cold air marks the crack before the shape does, drifting down the tunnel. Something huge shifts its coils, waiting quiet in the dark. Then it strikes, teeth ringed and hungry. Your rapier finds hide.

The path drops past a bottleneck thick with old spore dust and silence. A shape uncoils from shadow, gray and ancient beyond counting. It hunts by the sound of dripping water alone. Its bite lands fast.
Deep grooves mark stone where something huge has crawled for years, patient as the dark around it. It rears now, tentacles testing first. Teeth ring wide as a shield. You brace hard and strike back.

A wet clatter of loose stone rolls down the ridge behind you. Something ancient turns toward the sound, gray hide catching the dying torchlight. Its jaws snap once, testing distance. You raise your blade and hold your breath.

The ceiling looks like ordinary stone until it billows outward. A cloaker unfolds from the dark, wide as a sail, edges rippling. Its maw gapes and a moan rolls through the cavern. You raise your blade before it drops on you.

Ichor drips slow from a crack no wider than your hand. Something breathes above, waiting for footsteps to pass beneath its folds. You freeze mid step, heart hammering. The shape never moves first, only watches and counts.
A low moan rattles your teeth and the whole cavern seems to tilt sideways. It is only a trick, a false note meant to make you flee in terror. You grit your jaw and hold your ground against the sound.

The creature's tail cracks like a whip and wraps hard around your ribs. Pain spasms through you as it hauls you toward its gaping, ringed maw. You twist free half a second before the jaws finally close.

Stone dust sifts down from a crack shaped all wrong for plain stone. The cloaker's edge ripples, cape like, catching torchlight in oily bands of black. You back away slowly, never taking your eyes off it.
A missile of force streaks past your shoulder and slaps into cloth that was never cloth at all. The cloaker flinches hard, its folds snapping wide, showing a ringed, toothy underside beneath.
The moan comes again, closer now, and your knees nearly buckle from the raw dread it carries. You hum a steadying note under your breath instead. Fear washes off you like water off cold stone.
Somewhere above, something exhales slowly through a mouth full of crooked teeth. You do not look up, not once. You keep walking, counting steps, until the ceiling itself seems to breathe back at you.
Its wrap closes around your arm before you even see it move at all. Grappled, you feel your air run thin inside its heavy folds. You drive your blade up hard into its dark, wet seam.
The cavern narrows to a crossing barely wide enough for two abreast. A shadow drops from the low ceiling without a whisper of sound. You throw yourself sideways as its tail cracks the stone.
Your torch gutters and for a moment the whole ceiling seems to breathe with you. Then it billows outward, black and ringed, and a moan spills into the dark passage. You do not flinch, not this time.
The cloaker's tail hooks your ankle and drags you hard off balance. You slam your blade down into the leathery flesh beneath you. It lets go with a wet, ugly sound and folds back into shadow above.
A duet of humming notes rises against the moan, canceling its fear before it can take hold of your mind. The creature hisses, unused to prey that answers song with steady song of its own.
You spot the false ceiling too late, a pale seam of stone that should not be there at all. It drops without warning. You roll clear as its whole bulk slams the floor where you stood.
The maw closes around your shoulder, half swallowing you in a single vicious strike. You stab blind into the dark wet mouth until it releases you, spasming, and retreats back upward into the dark.
A single drip echoes wrong, too rhythmic, too patient to be plain water. You realize too late it was never water at all, only the cloaker counting its own held breath just above you.
The creature's underside ripples with teeth arranged in three overlapping rings. You drive your dagger through the nearest seam and it screams, folding hard in on itself.
Its tail lashes twice before you can even react, the second blow knocking your shield wide open. You duck under the third strike and put steel into the base of its wing.
A false note rings out from the deep dark, sweet and wrong, meant to draw you closer to the ceiling. You cover your ears and back away until the illusion of a friendly voice finally fades.
The cloaker drops from a crack barely wider than a coin, unfurling far wider than you thought possible. Its shadow swallows the torchlight whole for one long, terrible heartbeat.
You feel the grapple before you even see it, cold folds closing round your chest like a fist. Your breath goes thin and sour fast. You wedge your blade into the seam and twist hard.
The moan this time carries real menace, a low frequency that scrapes behind your eyes and lingers. You answer with a hummed counter chord and the dread simply falls away from you.
Blood and ichor mix on the cave floor, dark and glistening under flickering torchlight. The cloaker retreats along the ceiling, dragging its wounded tail behind it slowly.
A billowing shape peels off the rock overhead, and for one terrible second it looks exactly like a cloak someone dropped there. Then it opens its wide, ringed mouth wide.
The creature grapples your leg and hauls upward, lifting you clean off the stone floor entirely. You stab down through its heavy folds until it finally drops you, hissing loud.
Your ally's blade flashes past you and opens a long gash along the cloaker's flank. Ichor sprays dark across the cave wall in a wide arc. The creature shrieks and recoils fast.
The false ceiling notices you before you ever notice it, a subtle wrongness in the drip pattern above your head. You step back just as the whole slab of shadow falls hard.
A tail strike catches your shoulder and spins you half around in place. You catch your balance on the cavern wall and drive your blade backward into the waiting dark.
The moan wraps around the cavern like fog, pressing cold dread into every corner of the dark. You steady your grip and remember it is only sound, only a trick of fear.
Something wide and black detaches from the low ceiling above the narrow crossing ahead. It falls without a whisper, and only your shield saves you from its snapping jaws.
The creature's grip loosens as your blade finally finds the seam between its rings. Ichor washes over your hand, warm and foul smelling, and it releases you at last.
A cairn of loose stone marks where the last party fell, tail marks scored deep into the rock beside it still. You step carefully now, watching every shadow above your head.
The cloaker's underside gapes wide enough to swallow your whole arm in one bite. You brace your feet and drive your dagger straight up into its soft, ringed throat.
Two folds of shadow peel slowly apart on the ceiling, revealing rows of teeth where you expected only plain stone. You throw yourself flat as the jaws snap shut above you.
The moan hits like a physical blow, and for a moment your hands forget how to hold a blade at all. You breathe through it steadily, and your grip returns firm and sure.
A wet slap sounds behind you as the cloaker drops from where it clung, folds snapping open like a sail catching hard wind. You spin and meet it blade first, steady.
Ichor trails along the ceiling in a long dark smear, marking where the wounded creature dragged itself away to hide. You follow the trail with your torch raised high.
The creature wraps its tail twice around your waist and squeezes slowly until your ribs ache badly. You drive your elbow hard into its flank and it finally lets go.
A crack in the stone above you widens slowly, far too slowly to be natural at all. You step back just as the cloaker unfurls and drops into the space you left behind.
The moan fades into a low hum, almost musical, almost soothing, and that gentleness is exactly what makes it dangerous. You shake your head clear and press on anyway.
Your blade opens a long seam in its folded flank and thick ichor pours out dark as tar. The cloaker shrieks and thrashes, dragging itself back toward the high ceiling.
The false stone above you ripples once, betraying itself at last, and you throw a dagger before it can fully drop. It shrieks and retreats along the cave roof fast.
A cold gust rolls down the narrow passage, smelling of wet leather and old dried blood. Somewhere close, wide folds shift against stone, patient and utterly unseen.
The cloaker grapples your sword arm, forcing your blade wide and useless at your side. You headbutt its ringed flesh and it recoils just long enough for you to break free.
A shape like a torn cape hangs motionless on the ceiling until your torchlight passes slowly beneath it. Then it drops, and the whole cavern fills with its rising moan.
Your ally hums a bardic note that steadies your nerve just as the moan rolls heavy over you both. Fear washes past without ever taking hold, and you strike back hard.
The creature's tail cracks against your shield hard enough to numb your whole arm. You grit your teeth and drive forward, blade seeking the soft seam beneath its wing.
Loose stone clatters somewhere above, far too deliberate to be falling debris at all. You raise your shield a half second before the cloaker drops onto the narrow path.
Ichor spatters across your boots as your blade finally finds its mark deep in the ringed flesh above. The cloaker convulses once and releases its grip on your shoulder.
The moan dies slowly into silence, and for a moment you wonder if the fight is truly over. Then the shadow above shifts again, folds tightening for another sudden strike.
A crooked seam of darkness runs along the low ceiling, following you step for careful step. You do not run from it. You wait for it to commit, and then you strike first.
The cloaker's jaws close on empty air where your head had been just a heartbeat ago. You roll beneath it fast and drive your blade up through its soft, pale underbelly.
The forge glow leaks through cavern dark, throwing giant shadows on dripping stone. A hammer rings on the anvil, and the whole tunnel shakes. You grip your weapon and step slowly into the heat.
Molten light pools on the black chasm floor. The giant lifts a burning chain and swings it wide, sparks scattering across the wet stone. You duck low and feel the heat singe your hair.
Somewhere ahead a bellows breathes, slow and heavy, and the tunnel smells of hot iron and old smoke. A shape rises from the glow, taller than any door, and reaches slowly for its hammer.

Chains rattle against the cave wall, dragged by a huge hand. The fire giant steps from the forge light, its knee cracking the stone floor. Heat rolls off it like a furnace door left open. You raise your blade.
A hammer arcs down and buries itself in stone, missing you by inches. Sparks fly and the giant roars, spinning for another swing, chain dragging sparks behind it. You dart sideways into the dark.
The cavern floor is littered with old iron racks, ruined by heat. A giant kneels at the anvil, humming low, then looks up, eyes catching the forge glow, and the fight begins in earnest.

Deep in the dark a furnace burns without smoke rising, swallowed whole by stone. The giant grips a chain, swinging it like a whip. You brace on the slick floor as the length of it cracks past your ear.

Heat presses hard against your face before you even see the fire. A giant stands over a broken anvil, hammer raised high, eyes glowing like coals. You step forward, weapon ready, and hold your breath.

The tunnel narrows, hot air pushing past you like breath from some vast animal. A giant blocks the passage, chains looped across its shoulder, teeth bright against soot dark skin. You grip tight and charge.
Somewhere a hammer strikes true, ringing through the dark like a bell tolling twice. The fire giant turns, molten light spilling from its open palms. You raise your blade and hold your ground.
A rusted manacle still hangs from the giant's wrist, dragging an old broken chain behind it. It swings the length of it low at your knees. You leap the arc and feel the heat of it pass just below.
The chasm below glows faint orange, lit from some deeper furnace far under the stone. The giant looms at its edge, hammer resting on one shoulder, and turns slowly, grinning, stepping toward you.

Smoke curls from a crack in the stone ceiling, though there is no fire to see yet. Then the giant rounds the corner, forge light dancing bright on its arms and shoulders. You steady your grip and wait.
A meat rack stands rusted and empty near the forge, swinging slightly in the hot draft that pours from deeper stone. The giant notices you, drops its tongs, and reaches instead for its hammer.

The giant's palms glow faint orange, like coals cupped carefully in its huge hands. It presses them flat to the stone wall and the rock cracks wide open. You brace for whatever climbs through the gap.
Your torch gutters in the hot wind pouring from the tunnel ahead. A giant steps into the light, chain dragging behind it, and the whole floor seems to hum and shudder with contained heat.
A row of old iron tools hangs from hooks along the cavern wall, blackened by decades of forge smoke. The giant grabs the largest hammer off its hook and turns toward you with a slow, wide grin.
The stone floor is warm even before you catch sight of the fire giant. It kneels by the forge, tending flame with a huge careful hand, then rises slow and full, blocking the only way forward.
Somewhere below, metal clangs against metal in a steady rhythm you can feel through the floor. You round a bend and find the fire giant mid swing, hammer glowing white against the anvil.
A chain snaps taut across the passage, pulled by unseen weight somewhere in the dark. The fire giant steps from behind a boulder, forge light throwing its shadow twenty feet across the stone.
The cavern air shimmers with heat long before any light appears around the bend. Then the giant rounds it, dragging a burning chain that hisses and spits against the wet stone floor beneath it.
A broken anvil lies half buried in old ash near the chasm edge, cold now but scarred deep. The fire giant crouches beside it, running a thumb along the crack, then looks up and smiles wide.
Your boots hiss faintly on stone still warm from some earlier blast of heat further down. The giant emerges from a side tunnel, chain slung over one shoulder, molten light cupped in its palm.
The tunnel opens into a wide chamber ringed with old forge racks, all scorched black by decades of use. A giant stands at the center, hammer raised, and brings it down hard enough to crack stone.
Somewhere a bellows wheezes, pumping heat through the dark like a slow and patient heartbeat. Then a huge shape steps into view at last, chains dragging bright sparks across the stone floor.
A single ember drifts past your face, glowing faint against the black air of the tunnel. You look up to find the fire giant already watching, hammer resting easy on one massive shoulder.
The cave walls sweat with heat, water hissing softly where it meets the warm stone underfoot. A giant rounds the corner dragging a length of glowing chain, grinning wide at the sound of your steps.
Heat rises in slow waves from a wide crack in the cavern floor ahead of you. The fire giant climbs through it, shoulders scraping stone, then straightens to its full and terrible height.
A hammer blow rings out somewhere ahead, shaking loose dust from the low ceiling above you. You round the bend to find the giant mid swing, molten sparks arcing bright off the anvil's edge.
The forge fire throws long shadows across cracked stone, and among them something huge is moving. The fire giant steps forward, chain rattling low, and the whole tunnel seems to hold its breath.
A discarded gauntlet, still faintly smoking, lies abandoned near the chasm edge nearby. Its owner is not far behind. The fire giant rounds the bend, palms glowing orange, hunting only by sound.
The dark hums with distant hammer strikes, steady and even as a drum somewhere deep. You follow the sound to a wide chamber where the fire giant stands over its forge, patiently waiting.
Sparks drift slowly up from a crack in the floor like a swarm of lazy fireflies. Then a huge hand grips the stone edge, and the fire giant hauls itself up into the light, grinning wide.
The cavern smells of hot iron and old smoke, thick enough that you can nearly taste it. Somewhere close a chain drags heavy across stone. The fire giant steps into your torchlight, hammer ready.
A chunk of molten rock drops from the ceiling and hisses loudly in a puddle nearby. You look up as the fire giant descends a narrow ledge above, chain trailing sparks behind its huge frame.
The chasm glows faint red far at its bottom, like a coal bed banked low and waiting. The fire giant crouches at its edge, testing the depth with one boot, then turns and finds you standing there.
Somewhere a chain drags slow across stone, link by heavy link, closer with every breath. You round a corner and the fire giant is already there, hammer raised, forge light on its arms.
The air grows hot and dry as you push deeper into the black dark ahead of you. A giant blocks the tunnel, chain coiled at its feet, hammer resting easy on one broad and scarred shoulder.
A cracked furnace door swings loose on one hinge nearby, leaking orange light into the black. The fire giant steps through it, ducking low under the frame, and rises to its full terrible height.
Water drips somewhere overhead, hissing faintly where it lands on warm and waiting stone. The fire giant looks up from its forge, sees you standing there, and grins around a mouthful of soot.
A hammer the size of a door leans against the cavern wall, still glowing faintly at its head. Its owner returns from the deeper dark, chain dragging loud, and reaches slowly for the handle.
The tunnel floor is scorched black in a wide ring surrounding the forge ahead. A fire giant stands at its center, molten light pooling bright in its open palms. It steps heavy toward you.
Somewhere deep a bellows pumps, slow and steady, feeding a fire you cannot yet see around the bend. Then the giant rounds it, chains rattling loose, and the light finally reaches your face.
A broken chain link glows faint on the stone floor, still warm from recent use nearby. You follow its trail to a wide chamber where the fire giant waits, hammer resting easy on its shoulder.
The chasm wall glows faint orange where old lava has long hardened into black stone. A giant emerges from a crack in it, brushing dust from massive shoulders, and turns slow to face you.
A row of manacles hangs empty along the cave wall, rusted clean through by time and heat. The fire giant steps past them without a glance, chain dragging sparks, hunting the sound of your steps.
Heat blooms suddenly in the dark, like a furnace door swung open somewhere close by. Then the giant appears at last, hammer glowing white hot, and swings it down hard toward the stone floor.
The stone beneath your boots grows warmer with every single step deeper into the tunnel. A giant waits at the passage end, forge light burning behind it, chain coiled tight and ready.
A cracked anvil rests beside a cooling puddle of half hardened molten rock nearby. The fire giant kneels there, running a huge hand across the metal, then rises slow at the sound of your steps.
The dark presses close around you, lit only by a faint orange glow somewhere ahead. You round the bend and the fire giant is there waiting, chain in hand, hammer resting against the wall.

Somewhere a hammer strikes stone instead of metal, cracking the floor wide open in one blow. Dust and sparks rise together in the dark. The fire giant steps through the gap, grinning at what it finds.
A thin seam of lava glows steady along the cavern floor, tracing a path toward the dark ahead of you. Following it carefully, you find the fire giant already waiting, hammer already swinging down.
The cavern narrows into a black throat of water. Something vast uncoils beneath the surface, patient as stone. Mucus sheens the walls where it has passed. You grip your weapon and stop breathing.
A chime rings off the wet stone, too clean for this place. The water heaves once, twice, then goes still. Pale eyes surface first, wide and unblinking. Whatever waits below is not hungry yet.
Your torch gutters near the flooded ledge. Something enormous shifts beneath the film of slime on the water. It watches you the way a lake watches a stone before it sinks. You do not move.
The reek of filth and old fish hits you first. Then the water breaks, and a shape older than the tunnels rises. Its mouth is a ring of teeth around nothing. Your heart forgets its rhythm.
Dripping stone gives way to a flooded chamber. A voice slides into your skull that was never spoken aloud. Fifteen feet down, something vast is grinning. You back toward the dry stair.
A whisper touches your mind before you see it. The water glows faintly where its body coats the rock in slime. You feel your own thoughts start to soften and blur. You dig in your heels.
The tunnel opens onto a sunken lake, black and patient. Bubbles rise where nothing should be breathing. A shape the size of a wagon rolls once beneath the surface. Your torch shakes.
Rubbery flesh brushes the ledge beside your boot. You do not look down fast enough to see what left the trail. The water is glass-still again, waiting. Somewhere below, something waits too.
Cold air rolls off the flooded cavern ahead. A chord of sound hums through the stone, not quite a song. The water ripples though nothing touched it. You realize the ripple is aimed at you.
The ledge crumbles slightly under your weight. Below, pale shapes drift like driftwood that breathes. One eye opens, vast and lidless, fixed on you. The whisper in your skull grows louder.
Slime coats the tunnel floor in a long wet smear. It leads down toward black water that never stops moving. Something huge is testing the shallows, patient and unhurried. You raise your blade.
The cavern breathes with the tide of some hidden sea. A shape uncoils from the dark, longer than your torchlight reaches. Its skin shines with a film like old glass. You feel watched.
Water slaps softly against stone that should be dry. Something below stirs the surface without breaking it. A voice, not your own, counts your heartbeats. You refuse to listen closer.
The passage floods your boots to the ankle. Ahead, the black water swells like something breathing beneath it. A pale eye rises, blinks once, and studies you. Your grip tightens on the hilt.
Fungus glows faint blue along the flooded wall. The light catches on a trail of mucus thick as rope. It leads into water too still to be empty. Somewhere close, minds are being counted.
A low whisper slides through your thoughts uninvited. The lake ahead ripples though the air is dead calm. Something the size of a boulder is moving beneath it. You steady your breathing.
The cave floor tilts down into black, patient water. Bubbles rise slow and deliberate, like a held breath. A shape vast and pale drifts just beneath the surface. Your torchlight barely reaches it.
Wet stone gives way underfoot near the flooded shelf. Below, something ancient shifts its bulk without hurry. The whisper returns, softer this time, almost kind. You grip your blade tighter.
The chamber smells of filth and standing water. A ripple crosses the lake though nothing broke the surface. Somewhere below, a vast mind is already reading yours. You force yourself forward.
Your boots splash through an inch of black water. Ahead the cavern swells into a flooded dome, dripping and vast. Something huge turns lazily beneath the surface. The whisper in your skull sharpens.
A trail of slime gleams under your torchlight. It winds toward a lake so still it looks solid. Then the surface breaks, and a pale eye finds you. You do not blink first.
The tunnel drips steadily into a hidden pool. Something down there is patient, testing your resolve. A whisper counts your fears one by one. You answer with your blade instead.
Cold water laps at a ledge slick with mucus. Below, a vast shape rolls once, unhurried and confident. Its mind brushes yours like a cold hand. You push the thought away hard.
The cavern opens into a lake black as ink. Bubbles rise in slow, deliberate rhythm from below. Something the size of a small ship is watching you breathe. You do not look away.
Slime trails glisten along the flooded passage floor. They converge at a pool too calm to be natural. A whisper slides in, testing the seams of your mind. You clench your jaw and hold firm.
The chamber floods ankle deep in murky water. A ripple spreads though the surface should be still. Something vast and old is deciding whether you are worth its time. You raise your weapon first.
Your torch throws long shadows across dripping stone. Ahead, black water swells like a held breath. A pale eye opens beneath the surface, patient and huge. The whisper in your skull grows bold.
The passage narrows toward a flooded, glowing cavern. Fungus lights the water an eerie blue-green. Something enormous shifts its coils beneath the glow. You feel your thoughts start to drift.
Mucus streaks the wall where something huge has passed. It leads to a lake still as glass and twice as cold. A whisper slides into your mind, testing, patient. You answer only with silence.
The cave breathes with the slow tide of black water. Something vast rolls beneath the surface without breaking it. A voice not your own counts down your resolve. You grip the hilt tighter.
Water drips steadily from a ceiling thick with slime. Below, the lake ripples though nothing touched it. A pale eye rises slow and huge and patient. Your heart hammers against your ribs.
The flooded chamber glows faint with fungal light. Somewhere beneath, a vast mind is already testing yours. Bubbles rise in a slow, deliberate rhythm. You steady your grip and wait.
Your boots slide on mucus-slick stone near the ledge. Below, the water swells like something vast is breathing. A whisper touches your thoughts, cold and patient. You push forward anyway.
The tunnel opens onto black water, still as glass. Something huge shifts beneath it, testing the shallows. A voice slides into your skull uninvited. You answer by drawing your blade.
Slime coats the cavern floor in a wet, gleaming trail. It leads toward a lake too calm to trust. A pale eye opens beneath the surface, vast and patient. You do not step back.
The chamber floods with cold, black, patient water. Something ancient rolls beneath it without hurry. A whisper counts your heartbeats one by one. You grip your weapon and hold your ground.
Fungal light glows faint along the flooded tunnel wall. Ahead, the water ripples though the air is still. Something vast is testing your resolve from below. You do not flinch.
Your torch gutters near a ledge slick with slime. Below, a shape older than the caverns shifts lazily. A whisper slides in, soft and testing. You answer with steel instead of words.
The lake ahead is black, still, and utterly patient. Bubbles rise slow from something vast beneath the surface. A voice not your own brushes against your thoughts. You steady your stance.
Water laps the flooded ledge, slick with old mucus. Something huge is testing the shallows, unhurried and confident. A whisper counts down your resolve. You grip the hilt and wait.
The cavern narrows toward a pool glowing faint blue. Fungus lights the water like something alive underneath. A pale eye opens, vast and lidless, fixed on you. You raise your weapon first.
Slime trails lead down toward black, patient water. Something ancient shifts its bulk beneath the surface. A whisper slides into your mind, cold and testing. You force the thought back out.
The chamber drips steadily into a hidden, flooded pool. Below, something vast rolls without breaking the surface. A voice counts your fears one by one. You answer only with silence.
Your boots splash through cold, dripping cavern water. Ahead, the lake swells like a held, patient breath. Something huge is watching you from beneath the surface. You do not step back.
The tunnel floods ankle-deep near a glowing pool. Fungus casts an eerie blue light across the water. Something vast shifts beneath it, testing your resolve. You grip your blade tighter.
Mucus gleams along the wet stone near the ledge. It leads toward a lake still as glass and twice as cold. A whisper touches your mind, patient and testing. You push the thought away hard.
The cave breathes with the slow tide of black water. A pale eye rises slow and huge beneath the surface. A voice not your own counts your heartbeats. You steady your grip and hold firm.
Water drips from a ceiling thick with fungal glow. Below, something ancient tests the shallows, unhurried. A whisper slides into your skull uninvited. You answer with drawn steel.
The flooded chamber glows faint with eerie blue light. Somewhere beneath, a vast mind studies yours calmly. Bubbles rise slow and deliberate from below. You raise your weapon and wait.
Your boots slide on slime near a flooded ledge. Below, the water swells like something vast is breathing. A whisper counts down your resolve, cold and patient. You push forward anyway.
The tunnel opens onto black, glassy, patient water. Something huge tests the shallows beneath the surface. A voice slides into your skull, testing your will. You answer by raising your blade.
Slime coats the cavern floor in a gleaming, wet trail. It leads toward a pool too calm to be trusted. A pale eye opens beneath the surface, vast and old. You do not step back.
11: The cavern floor drops toward a bottleneck barely wide enough for one body. Deep grooves mark the stone where something huge has crawled for years, patient and unseen. You catch the smell of old ichor before you hear the drip, and your torch finds a ring of teeth already too close, tentacles first.

Somewhere past the chasm a stalagmite cracks and topples, the sound rolling through the gallery. Six legs answer at once, clicking closer over wet stone. The grick ancient uncoils from behind a fallen slab, gray hide catching torchlight, teeth ringing wide. You brace and strike where the sound was loudest.

The bottleneck forces you shoulder to shoulder with the black, ceiling dripping slow like a clock. A shape shifts along the ridge above, testing the air with a tentacle before it drops. Its bite lands solid on unblunted armor, steaming ichor beading where your rapier finally answers.

Deep grooves worn smooth lead you into a gallery ringed with old claw marks and older blood. Something ancient waits past the fallen ladder, gray and huge, hide thick as bark. It rears without warning, tentacles first, teeth ringed wide enough to swallow a shield. You barely raise your blade in time.

The chasm narrows to a single crossing of loose, slick stone, and something coils beneath it. The moment your boot lands the whole ledge shudders, and a shape the size of a cart rears from the dark. Its tentacles lash wide before its teeth even find the light. You swing back and feel the blade skid off hide.

A soft drip becomes a drum becomes a roar deep in the gallery, and the floor shakes as something huge shifts its coils. Torchlight catches a ring of teeth wide as a shield, gray hide steaming where old ichor never dried. There is no time left to retreat, only to meet the lunge with your own.

The path drops past a bottleneck thick with old spore dust, glowcap puffing pale clouds with every step. Something ancient uncoils from the shadow beyond, hunting by the sound of dripping water alone. Its bite lands before your guard is up, teeth ringing hard against your armor. You answer with steel.

Cold air pours from a crack wide enough for one body, carrying the smell of rot and older stone. Something huge fills the gap, teeth first, and you hear six legs click on rock before torchlight finds its shape. It anchors like it has always lived there, then strikes without a sound of warning.

Deep in the dark something drags itself along a groove worn smooth by years of hunting, gray hide catching stray light. It tests the air with a tentacle, teeth ringed wide and hungry in the gloom. The strike lands on your shoulder before you can brace, steaming ichor smeared across old scars in the rock.

The gallery smells of old blood and wet fungus long before the shape reveals itself along the ridge. A cart-sized coil unwinds slow, hunting the sound of your held breath rather than sight. Its teeth ring wide when it finally opens its mouth, and the first bite lands solid despite your guard.

A crack of falling rock announces something ancient waking past the ladders, patient as the stone it has slept in for years. Gray hide fills the tunnel mouth, tentacles testing the cold air between you. Its teeth ring wider than your shield, and the first strike comes fast. You barely hold your ground.

The ridge above drips slow and steady, marking time like a clock counting down. Something long uncoils from a crack in the stone, hunting the sound of your steps rather than any torchlight. Its bite lands hard on unblunted armor before you can raise your guard. Steaming ichor marks where you struck back.

Somewhere past the ladders a stone shelf groans and finally gives way, dropping loose rubble into the black. Six legs grip the dark like it is home, clicking closer toward the sound of your breath. A ring of teeth opens on a roar, and you raise your blade against the coming lunge, ichor answering at last.

The floor is slick with something worse than water, dark and old, pooled in the low grooves of the stone. A gray shape rears from the ridge above, testing the air with tentacles before it commits. Its lash comes in a wide, sudden arc that catches you by surprise. You duck low and swing into the opening.

Cold air marks the crack before the shape does, drifting down the tunnel long minutes ahead of any threat. Something huge shifts its coils in the dark, waiting quiet, patient as stone that has not moved in years. Then it strikes, teeth ringed and hungry, hide catching the last of your dying torchlight.

The path drops past a bottleneck thick with old spore dust and a silence that feels wrong even here. A shape uncoils from shadow, gray and ancient beyond easy counting, hunting the sound of dripping water. Its bite lands fast, catching your guard half raised. Ichor spatters warm as you finally answer.

Deep grooves mark stone where something huge has crawled for years, patient as the dark surrounding it. It rears now from behind a fallen slab, tentacles testing the air first before committing. Teeth ring wide as a shield, catching the last of your torchlight. You brace hard and strike back.

A stalagmite cracks loose somewhere in the black, and the sound draws an answer at once from the gallery. Six legs click closer, hunting by ear alone across wet, uneven stone. Its hide shrugs off the first glancing hit like nothing more than a scratch. Ichor finally beads at the second strike.

The chasm edge crumbles underfoot, loose gravel skidding fast into the dark where something ancient waits, coiled and patient. Roused by the noise, it lunges hard, tentacles first, faster than its size should allow. You barely catch your footing and swing into the opening its reach just left behind.

Somewhere in the gallery a torch gutters against a sudden cold draft with no obvious source. Six legs click a patient rhythm against stone, closing in slow past the bottleneck and the old claw marks scored deep. A ring of teeth opens wide, hungry and unhurried. You strike where the sound was loudest.

The cavern narrows past a ledge slick with dried ichor and older blood that never quite washed away. A gray shape rears up from behind the ridge, ancient and huge, tentacles coiling before the lash begins. It strikes without warning, catching your shoulder hard. Your blade meets hide in answer.

A low roar shakes loose stone somewhere past the chasm, patient and huge, rolling through the tight gallery. Six legs grip the ridge like it weighs nothing, closing the distance fast. Its bite comes aimed square at your unblunted armor, testing the seams. You brace and answer, ichor spattering warm.

The gallery smells of wet fungus and something worse underneath, a rot that clings to the back of your throat. A shape the size of a cart uncoils from shadow, hunting your breath rather than any light you carry. Its teeth ring wide, closing the last few feet fast. You swing first, and steaming ichor answers.

Deep in the dark something drags itself along a groove worn smooth by years of patient hunting, hide catching stray torchlight like wet slate. Tentacles lash before you can flinch, testing your guard before committing. Ichor answers your blade when it finally connects against the cold stone floor.

The chasm narrows to one crossing of loose, slick stone, barely wide enough for careful footing in the dripping dark. Something huge coils beneath it, waiting on the sound of feet rather than any light. It strikes the moment you land, tentacles first. You catch your balance and swing back hard.

A crack of falling rock wakes something ancient in the tunnel mouth, rousing it from a stillness that had fooled your torchlight. Gray hide fills the passage almost at once, tentacles testing the cold air. Its teeth ring wider than your shield, hungry and sudden. You hold ground and strike first.

The ridge above drips slow, marking time like a clock in the dark gallery where nothing else moves for long stretches. Something long uncoils, hunting the sound of your steps rather than any light. Its bite lands hard on your armor before you can brace. Steaming ichor answers where you struck back.

Somewhere past the ladders a stone shelf groans and finally gives way, dropping loose rubble into a gap that swallows the sound whole. Below, six legs grip the dark like it is home to them, patient in a way that unsettles more than any roar could. You raise your blade, and steel meets the ancient hide.

The floor is slick with something worse than water, dark and old, pooling in low grooves that have held it for years. A gray shape rears from the ridge, testing the air with a tentacle before it fully commits. Its lash comes in a wide, sudden arc. You duck low and come up swinging hard into the gap.

Cold air marks the crack before the shape does, drifting down the tunnel minutes ahead of any real danger. Something huge shifts its coils, waiting quiet in the deep dark, patient as stone untouched for years. Then it strikes without warning, teeth ringed and hungry, catching the last of your torch.

The path drops past a bottleneck thick with old spore dust and a silence that feels wrong even for this deep dark. A shape uncoils from shadow, gray and ancient beyond easy counting, hunting the sound of water alone. Its bite lands fast, catching your guard only half raised. Ichor spatters warm across old stone.

Deep grooves mark stone where something huge has crawled for years, patient as the endless dark surrounding it. It rears now from behind a fallen slab, tentacles testing the cold air first. Teeth ring wide as a shield, catching what little torchlight remains. You brace hard and strike back at last.

The ceiling of the passage looks like plain stone until it billows outward like a torn cape catching wind. A cloaker unfolds from the crack above, wide and ringed and hungry, and a moan rolls through the cavern that makes your knees waver. You steady your grip, raise your blade, and refuse to run.

Ichor drips slow and dark from a crack no wider than your hand, and something above breathes with the patience of a creature that has waited a long time for footsteps to pass beneath it. You freeze mid step, torch trembling, and notice too late that the shape on the ceiling has already noticed you first, folds tightening for the strike.

A low moan rattles your teeth and the whole cavern seems to tilt sideways, stone and shadow swapping places for one sick heartbeat. It is only a trick, a false note pitched to make you flee screaming into the dark. You grit your jaw, hum a steadying answer under your breath, and hold your ground against the sound until it finally breaks.

The creature's tail cracks like a whip and wraps hard around your ribs, cold and leathery and impossibly strong. Pain spasms through you as it hauls you toward its gaping, ringed maw, teeth clicking eager in three overlapping rows. You twist against the grip, driving your blade into the seam behind its wing, and it finally lets go.

Stone dust sifts down from a crack shaped all wrong for plain stone, drifting through your torchlight like falling ash. The cloaker's edge ripples along the ceiling, cape like, catching the flame in oily bands of black and grey. You back away slowly, never taking your eyes off it, until your shoulder finds the narrow crossing behind you.

A missile of pure force streaks past your shoulder and slaps into cloth that was never cloth at all, and the illusion breaks apart in an instant. The cloaker flinches hard, its folds snapping wide open, showing a ringed and toothy underside no one in the party had expected. You press the advantage before it can fold itself shut again.

The moan comes again, closer now, low and aching, and your knees nearly buckle from the raw dread it carries through the stone. You hum a steadying note under your breath instead of answering the fear, and it washes off you like water off cold rock. Somewhere in the dark, the creature hisses at prey that refuses to break.

Somewhere above, something exhales slowly through a mouth full of crooked, overlapping teeth, patient as the dripping stone around you. You do not look up, not once, though every instinct screams at you to run. You keep walking, counting steps, until the ceiling itself seems to breathe back at you in the dark, and then you strike.

Its wrap closes around your arm before you even see it move, cold folds tightening fast around muscle and bone. Grappled, you feel your air run thin inside its heavy embrace, lungs straining against the crush. You drive your blade up hard into its dark, wet seam, and warm ichor spills over your knuckles as it finally releases you.

The cavern narrows to a crossing barely wide enough for two abreast, walls glistening with old damp and older blood. A shadow drops from the low ceiling without a whisper of sound, wider than any cloak has a right to be. You throw yourself sideways as its tail cracks stone where your skull had been only a heartbeat before.

Your torch gutters low, and for a moment the whole ceiling seems to breathe along with your ragged breathing. Then it billows outward, black and ringed, and a moan spills into the passage thick enough to taste. You do not flinch this time, remembering the trick for what it is, and you meet the falling shadow with steel raised.

The cloaker's tail hooks your ankle and drags you hard off balance, scraping your shoulder against jagged stone. You slam your blade down into the leathery flesh coiled beneath you, twisting the edge deep. It lets go with a wet, ugly sound and folds itself back into the shadow overhead, dragging a thin trail of ichor behind it.

A duet of humming notes rises against the creature's moan, weaving together to cancel the fear before it can take root. The cloaker hisses, unused to prey that answers dread with steady song rather than screaming flight. You press forward while it is confused, blade seeking the seam beneath its folds.

You spot the false ceiling only a heartbeat too late, a pale seam of stone that never should have been there among the honest rock. It drops without warning, filling the passage with sudden black bulk and the reek of old blood. You roll clear just as its whole weight slams the floor where you had been standing.

The maw closes around your shoulder, half swallowing you in one vicious, ringed strike that drives the breath from your lungs. You stab blind into the dark wet mouth again and again until it releases you, spasming, and retreats back upward into the crack it came from. Ichor drips after it, marking a trail along the stone.

A single drip echoes wrong in the dark, too rhythmic and too patient to be plain water finding its way through stone. You realize too late it was never water at all, only the cloaker above counting its own held breath while it waited. By the time you understand, its folds are already unfurling directly overhead.

The creature's underside ripples with teeth arranged in three overlapping rings, each one clicking faintly as it shifts its grip on the ceiling. You drive your dagger through the nearest seam and it screams, a sound too human for comfort, folding hard in on itself as ichor sprays across the cavern wall in a wide dark arc.

Its tail lashes twice before you can even react, the second blow knocking your shield wide open and leaving your side exposed. You duck under the third strike, feel the wind of it pass your ear, and put steel into the base of its wing where the leather runs thinnest. The creature shudders and finally begins to retreat.

A false note rings out from the deep dark, sweet and wrong, meant to draw you closer to the waiting ceiling above. You cover your ears, remembering every warning about illusions in this stretch of the Underdark, and back away until the pretense of a friendly voice finally fades into silence and dripping stone.

The cloaker drops from a crack barely wider than a coin, unfurling far wider than you thought anything could fold itself into. Its shadow swallows the torchlight whole for one long, terrible heartbeat, and the moan that follows seems to come from everywhere at once. You brace your feet and wait for it to commit to the strike.

You feel the grapple before you even see it, cold folds closing round your chest like a fist tightening slowly. Your breath goes thin and sour fast, lungs burning against the pressure, and for a moment panic threatens to take you. You wedge your blade into the seam between its rings and twist hard until it finally lets go.

The moan this time carries real menace, a low frequency that scrapes behind your eyes and lingers long after the sound fades. You answer with a hummed counter chord, steady and low, and the dread simply falls away from your shoulders like a cloak dropped to the floor. The creature above hisses, clearly displeased by the failure.

Blood and ichor mix on the cave floor, dark and glistening under flickering torchlight, marking where the fight has already turned. The cloaker retreats along the ceiling, dragging its wounded tail behind it in a slow, uneven crawl. You do not chase it into the dark, content to let a wounded thing find somewhere else to hide.

A billowing shape peels off the rock overhead, and for one terrible second it looks exactly like a cloak someone dropped and forgotten there. Then it opens its wide, ringed mouth, teeth gleaming faintly in the torchlight, and the illusion shatters into something far more dangerous. You raise your blade and refuse to step back.

The creature grapples your leg and hauls upward, lifting you clean off the stone floor and dangling you above the drop. You stab down through its heavy folds again and again until it finally drops you, hissing loud enough to echo down the passage. You land hard but whole, blade still gripped tight in your hand.

Your ally's blade flashes past you and opens a long gash along the cloaker's flank, dark ichor spraying wide across the cave wall. The creature shrieks, a sound that scrapes at the edges of your hearing, and recoils fast toward the safety of the low ceiling. You press the advantage while it is still reeling from the wound.

The false ceiling notices you before you ever notice it, a subtle wrongness in the drip pattern that should have warned you sooner. You step back just as the whole slab of shadow falls where you had been standing a heartbeat before. Somewhere behind you, a familiar hummed note steadies your racing heart.

A tail strike catches your shoulder and spins you half around in the narrow passage, stone scraping your back raw. You catch your balance against the cavern wall and drive your blade backward into the waiting dark without even turning to look, trusting instinct over sight in a fight this close and this fast.

The moan wraps around the cavern like fog, pressing cold dread into every corner of the dark until even your torchlight seems to dim. You steady your grip and remind yourself it is only sound, only a trick meant to unravel your courage before the real attack ever lands. The creature above waits, patient, for you to break.

Something wide and black detaches from the low ceiling above the narrow crossing ahead, falling without so much as a whisper of sound. Only your shield saves you from its snapping jaws, the impact numbing your whole arm to the shoulder. You shove back hard, driving it away long enough to find your footing again.

The creature's grip loosens at last as your blade finally finds the seam between its rings, slipping deep into softer flesh beneath the leather. Ichor washes over your hand, warm and foul smelling, and it releases you all at once, retreating in a graceless scramble back toward the ceiling it fell from.

A cairn of loose stone marks where some earlier party fell, tail marks scored deep into the surrounding rock and old blood gone black with age. You step carefully now, watching every shadow above your head, unwilling to become another marker for the next unlucky traveler who passes through this crossing.

The tunnel opens into a forge chamber carved deep beneath the mountain, anvils lined along one wall like altars. A fire giant stands among them, hammer on its shoulder, molten light pooling in the seams of its skin. It rings the hammer once against stone, a challenge older than the cavern itself. You step forward, weapon raised.

Chains hang from hooks bolted into the ceiling, swaying in drafts that smell of hot iron. Somewhere below, a furnace breathes slow, feeding light into cracks across the black stone floor. The fire giant rises from tending it, shoulders scraping the low roof, and grins as the glow catches its eyes. You brace and grip your blade tighter.

A cracked anvil sits at the center of the chamber, scorched black from decades of use, and beside it kneels something far too large to be a person. The fire giant looks up slowly, eyes like twin coals in a soot streaked face. It rises without hurry, dragging a chain that hisses against warm stone. You do not wait for it to finish standing.

The chasm below glows faint orange at its bottom, a wound in the earth that never quite closes. Heat rolls up in slow waves, carrying the smell of hot metal and old smoke. A giant climbs from a crack near the edge, straightening to a height that blots out your torchlight. It grins at the sound of your footsteps and reaches for its hammer.

Somewhere deep a hammer strikes true again and again, ringing through stone like a bell that never stops. You follow the sound past racks of ruined iron tools until the chamber opens wide, forge fire throwing long shadows. The fire giant turns from its anvil, molten light spilling from cupped palms, and finds you in its doorway.

A thin seam of lava traces a glowing path along the cavern floor, winding deeper into black stone. Following it carefully, torch low, you round a bend into a chamber ringed with old forge racks. A fire giant stands at the center, chain coiled at its feet, hammer swinging up onto one shoulder. It grins, and the tunnel seems to hold its breath.

The stone beneath your boots grows warmer with every step deeper into the tunnel, the air thick and dry. At the passage end waits a shape too large for the space, forge light burning behind it in long orange bars. The fire giant steps forward, chain dragging sparks across wet stone, and brings its hammer down hard enough to crack the floor.

Water drips somewhere overhead, hissing faintly where it lands on stone gone warm from buried heat. You press past a rusted meat rack, hooks empty and swinging in the draft, until the tunnel opens into a forge chamber. The fire giant looks up from its anvil, sees you in the entrance, and grins around a mouthful of soot.

A broken gauntlet, still faintly smoking, lies abandoned near the edge of a wide black chasm. Its owner is close, heat already pressing against your face from somewhere just ahead. The fire giant rounds a boulder, palms glowing faint orange, hunting only by the sound of your steps. You grip your blade and refuse to give it an easy target.

The cavern smells of hot iron and old smoke long before any light appears around the bend. Somewhere close a chain drags heavy across stone, link by link, patient as a held breath. You round the corner and the fire giant is already there, hammer raised, forge light dancing on its scarred arms. There is no time left to retreat now.

Deep beneath the mountain a furnace burns without ever letting smoke escape, swallowed whole by stone. The fire giant tending it grips a chain in one huge fist, testing its weight before swinging it wide like a whip. You brace on the slick floor as the chain cracks past your ear, sparks scattering into the dark. The fight has only just begun.

A row of manacles hangs empty along the cave wall, rusted through by years of heat and damp. The fire giant steps past them without a glance, chain dragging sparks across the black stone floor. It hunts by sound alone down here, head tilted, and your footsteps have already given you away. You raise your weapon and step into the light.

Smoke curls lazily from a crack in the low stone ceiling, though no fire is yet visible in the dark ahead. Then the giant rounds a corner at last, forge light dancing across huge arms scarred by decades at the anvil. You steady your grip, feet planted on warm stone, and watch its eyes catch the glow like coals set in soot.

Somewhere a bellows wheezes steady in the dark, pumping heat through the tunnel like a slow heartbeat. You follow the sound past a broken anvil half buried in cold ash near a chasm's edge. A huge shape steps into view, chains dragging bright sparks across wet stone, and turns to face you with a grin carved from firelight and soot.

The chasm wall glows faint orange where old lava has hardened slowly into black, pitted stone. A giant emerges from a crack in it, brushing dust and rubble from massive shoulders, and turns to find you standing there with torch raised. It reaches for the hammer slung across its back without taking its glowing eyes off you.

Sparks drift slowly upward from a wide crack in the cavern floor, like a swarm of lazy fireflies. Then a huge hand grips the stone edge and hauls a fire giant up into the light, grinning at what it finds waiting. You grip your blade tighter as heat rolls off its skin in visible waves, thick as an open furnace door.

A cracked furnace door swings loose on one rusted hinge nearby, leaking bars of orange light into the black. The fire giant steps through it, ducking low under a frame far too small for its frame, and rises to its full terrible height once clear. It grins at the sight of you, hammer swinging up onto one broad shoulder.

The tunnel narrows until it barely fits two abreast, hot air pushing past you like breath from something enormous. A giant blocks the passage ahead, chains looped heavy across one shoulder, teeth bright against skin streaked dark with soot. You grip your weapon, plant your feet on the warm stone, and charge before it can finish rising.

Heat blooms suddenly in the dark, like a furnace door swung wide somewhere close by. Then the giant appears at last, hammer glowing white hot at its head, and swings it down hard toward the cracked stone floor. Dust and sparks rise together, and you throw yourself sideways before the blow can land near your feet.

A hammer the size of a door leans against the cavern wall nearby, its head still glowing faint from recent use. Its owner returns from somewhere deeper in the dark, chain dragging loud across wet stone, and reaches slowly for the handle without breaking stride. You watch the muscles bunch across its shoulder and ready your blade.

The dark hums with distant hammer strikes, steady as a drum beaten somewhere far below the stone. You follow the sound past old iron racks, ruined by decades of forge heat, until the chamber opens wide around you. The fire giant stands over its anvil, patient and waiting, molten light spilling slow from its cupped palms.

Somewhere a chain drags slow across stone, link by heavy link, drawing closer with every breath you take. You round a corner in the black tunnel and find the fire giant already there, hammer raised above its head, forge light dancing across its scarred arms. There is nowhere left to retreat, so you plant your feet and hold your ground.

A discarded chain link glows faint on the cavern floor, still warm from whatever it was dragged across. You follow its trail through narrowing stone until it opens into a chamber where the fire giant waits, hammer resting across one shoulder. It watches you approach, forge fire throwing its shadow across the cracked black floor.

The cave walls sweat steadily with heat, water hissing softly wherever it meets the warm stone underfoot. A giant rounds the corner ahead, dragging a length of glowing chain that hisses against the wet floor, grinning at the sound of your careful steps. You raise your blade and refuse to let the heat slow your advance.

Heat rises in slow waves from a wide crack splitting the cavern floor just ahead of your boots. The fire giant climbs through it unhurried, shoulders scraping jagged stone, then straightens to its full and terrible height once clear of the gap. It grins down at you, hammer already swinging up, and the chamber seems to hold its breath.

A single ember drifts lazily past your face, glowing faint against the black and heavy air of the tunnel. You look up slowly to find the fire giant already watching from the dark, hammer resting on one massive shoulder, eyes like coals set deep in soot. It does not wait for you to finish looking before it moves.

The forge fire throws long shadows across cracked and blackened stone, and among them something huge is already moving toward you. The fire giant steps forward, chain rattling low against the warm floor, and the tunnel seems to hold its breath around the two of you. You grip your weapon, plant your feet, and refuse to flinch first.

A broken anvil lies half buried in old cold ash near the edge of a wide black chasm, scarred deep from decades of hammer blows. The fire giant crouches beside it, running a thumb slowly along the crack, then looks up and smiles wide enough to show teeth stained dark with soot before reaching for its hammer.

Your boots hiss faintly on stone still warm from some earlier blast of heat deeper in the tunnel ahead. The giant emerges from a side passage, chain slung heavy over one shoulder, molten light cupped in its huge open palm like a coal it refuses to drop. It turns toward the sound of your steps and grins, already closing the gap.

Somewhere below, metal clangs against metal in a steady rhythm you can feel through the stone beneath your boots. You round a bend in the narrowing tunnel and find the fire giant mid swing, hammer glowing white hot against a cracked anvil. It sees you standing there, grins wide, and turns from its work to meet you.

The chasm glows faint red far down at its bottom, like a coal bed banked low and patient, waiting for something to feed it. The fire giant crouches at the edge, testing the depth with one huge boot, then turns and finds you standing there with your blade already raised. It grins and steps toward you across the warm stone.

A cracked anvil rests beside a cooling puddle of half hardened molten rock, steam still rising faintly from its edges. The fire giant kneels there, running a massive hand slowly across the pitted metal, then rises unhurried at the sound of your careful steps. It turns, hammer lifting, forge light catching every scar across its arms.

The cavern floor slopes into a sunken lake, black and still. Your torch touches the water, and something vast turns beneath it without breaking the surface. Mucus streaks the nearest rock. A whisper slides into your thoughts, patient and cold, and you refuse to answer it.

Dripping stone gives way to a flooded chamber where the air feels watched. Below the black water a shape older than the tunnels rolls lazily, testing the shallows. Pale eyes open and fix on you, unblinking. A voice never spoken slides into your skull, counting your fears one by one.

The tunnel narrows toward a pool lit faint blue by fungus on the walls. Bubbles rise slow and deliberate from something enormous below, patient as the stone itself. A rope of mucus trails the ledge where it last surfaced. Your thoughts start to soften, and you push the feeling back hard.

Cold air rolls off the flooded gallery, carrying the reek of filth and standing water. A chord of sound hums through the wet stone, not quite a song. The lake ripples though nothing visible touched it. Somewhere beneath, a vast mind has already noticed your fear and begun to test it.

The ledge crumbles slightly under your boots near the black water. Below, pale rubbery shapes drift like driftwood that breathes, rising and sinking. One vast eye opens, lidless and huge, fixed on you without hurry. The whisper in your skull grows bolder, testing the seams of your resolve.

Slime coats the tunnel floor, a wet trail leading toward water that never stops moving. Something huge tests the shallows, patient and unhurried, circling before it shows itself. You raise your blade and steady your stance. The cavern breathes with the slow tide of a hidden underground sea.

Water slaps softly against stone that should be bone dry this deep in the caverns. Something below stirs the black surface without ever breaking it. A voice not your own counts your heartbeats, slow and certain. You refuse to listen closer, though the whisper only grows louder.

The passage floods your boots as the tunnel opens onto a hidden lake. Ahead, the water swells like something enormous is breathing beneath it. A pale eye rises, blinks once, and studies you like a fisherman studies a line. Your grip tightens, and you step one pace closer instead of back.

Fungus glows faint blue along the flooded wall, light catching a trail of mucus thick as rope. It leads into water too still to be empty. Somewhere close beneath the surface, minds are being counted and sorted like coins. You steady your breathing and refuse to let the whisper choose.

The cave floor tilts down into black, patient water that swallows your torchlight whole. Bubbles rise slow and deliberate, like something enormous is holding its breath. A shape vast and pale drifts just beneath the surface, circling the chamber. Your torchlight barely reaches it, and the whisper grows insistent.

Wet stone gives way underfoot near the flooded shelf where old slime pools in every crack. Below, something ancient shifts its bulk without hurry. The whisper returns, softer this time, almost kind, and that frightens you most. You grip your blade tighter and hold your ground against the pull.

The chamber smells of filth and standing water, thick enough to taste. A ripple crosses the black lake though nothing visible broke the surface. Somewhere below, a vast and ancient mind is already reading yours, sorting through your fears. You force yourself forward anyway, refusing to let it win.

Your boots splash through cold black water as the cavern swells into a flooded dome overhead. Something huge turns lazily beneath the surface, entirely certain of its patience. The whisper in your skull sharpens, testing the edges of your resolve. You answer only with silence, gripping your weapon tighter.

A trail of slime gleams under your torchlight, winding toward a lake so still it looks solid. Then the surface breaks all at once, and a single pale eye rises to find you at the edge. You do not blink first, though your heart hammers hard. The whisper slides deeper, patient and cold, testing your fear.

The tunnel drips steadily into a hidden pool tucked behind a curtain of fungal light. Something down there is patient, testing your resolve like water testing a crack in stone. A whisper counts your fears one by one, never in any hurry. You answer with your blade instead, though every instinct screams to run.

Cold water laps at a ledge slick with old mucus, the smell of filth thick in the still air. Below, a vast shape rolls once, unhurried and entirely at ease in its flooded domain. Its mind brushes yours like a cold hand pressed against your skull. You push the thought away hard, gripping your weapon until it aches.

The cavern opens into a lake black as ink, swallowing your torchlight the moment it touches water. Bubbles rise slow and deliberate from something enormous moving beneath the surface. Something the size of a small ship is watching you breathe, patient and certain. You do not look away, though every part of you wants to run.
